author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: A fully believable, flesh-and-blood (albeit not human flesh and blood) romance is the beating heart of "Avatar." Cameron has never made a movie just to show off visual pyrotechnics: Every bit of technology in "Avatar" serves the greater purpose of a deeply felt love story.

author: Chris Hewitt (1)
quote: It's been twelve years since "Titanic," but the King of the World has returned with a flawed but fantastic tour de force that, taken on its merits as a film, especially in two dimensions, warrants four stars. However, if you can wrap a pair of 3D glasses round your peepers, this becomes a transcendent, full-on five-star experience that's the closest we'll ever come to setting foot on a strange new world. Just don't leave it so long next time, eh, Jim?

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Avatar is all-enveloping and transporting, with Cameron & Co.'s years of R&D paying off with a film that, as his work has done before, raises the technical bar and throws down a challenge for the many other filmmakers toiling in the sci-fi/fantasy realm.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Once again, [Cameron] has silenced the doubters by simply delivering an extraordinary film. There is still at least one man in Hollywood who knows how to spend $250 million, or was it $300 million, wisely.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The first 90 minutes of Avatar are pretty terrific - a full-immersion technological wonder with wonders to spare. The other 72 minutes, less and less terrific.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: As visual spectacle, Avatar is indelible, but as a movie it all but evaporates as you watch it.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Embrace the movie -- surely the most vivid and persuasive creation of a fantasy world ever seen in the history of moving pictures -- as a total sensory, sensuous, sensual experience.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: Avatar is a technological wonder, 15 years percolating in King Cameron's imagination and inarguably the greatest 3-D cavalry western ever made. Too bad that western is "Dances With Wolves."

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: The question lingers as the movie comes to its triumphant body-swapping close: Is this a pro-environment parable or a prophecy of virtual realities yet to come? Cameron's new world may very well be a verdant Matrix.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Tone-deaf but thunderously exciting.

author: Nick Starkey
quote: This is, simply put, one of the most beautiful movies you’ll ever see.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Avatar clears the hurdle in terms of being optical candy. Its story, though, is pure cheese.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Rarely less than absorbing and never boring over its nearly three-hour length.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The scenes in Pandora -- a planet with an Earth-like environment -- are so breathtaking that the narrative seems almost beside the point.

author: David Denby
quote: The movie's story may be a little trite, and the big battle at the end between ugly mechanical force and the gorgeous natural world goes on forever, but what a show Cameron puts on! The continuity of dynamized space that he has achieved with 3-D gloriously supports his trippy belief that all living things are one.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: It's thrilling and lovely and sad and explosive in all the right ways, and it needs to be seen – on the big screen, in 3-D – to be believed.

author: Ty Burr
quote: An entertainment to be not just seen but absorbed on a molecular level; it's as close to a full-body experience as we'll get until they invent the holo-suits. Cameron aims for sheer wonderment, and he delivers.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Avatar delivers. Combining beyond-state-of-the-art moviemaking with a tried-and-true storyline and a gamer-geek sensibility - not to mention a love angle, an otherworldly bestiary, and an arsenal of 22d-century weaponry - the movie quite simply rocks.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Titanic technical achievement.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Whatever faults Avatar may have -- and there are many -- the movie succeeds in immersing you in a photorealistic, painstakingly detailed world more fully than any science fiction movie before.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: A quantum leap in movie magic; watching it, I began to understand how people in 1933 must have felt when they saw "King Kong."

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Avatar's shock and awe demand to be seen. You've never experienced anything like it, and neither has anyone else.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: Avatar is a weak patchwork of his other films: the leaden voiceover from "Terminator 2" here, the military/civilian conflict from "Aliens" there, even a Jack-and-Rose-style forbidden love story cued to adult-contempo soundtrack.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: By presenting Avatar in 3-D, Cameron is staking his claim and building a fence around his own precious resource, making it unobtainable on any but his own terms to increasingly emboldened and technologically savvy natives.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Glorious and goofy and blissfully deranged.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Avatar is entertainment of the highest order. It's the best movie of 2009.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Much of the time, though, you're transfixed by the beauty of a spectacle that seems all of a piece. Special effects have been abolished, in effect, since the whole thing is so special.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Avatar is a king's ransom fairly well spent, not least because Cameron's invitation into his superbly crafted universe comes with an unexpected price: He makes it easy to gaze fondly on all this movie magic, but only in exchange for a hard look at ourselves.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: If I never felt entirely transported by Avatar, it's probably because the story thudded just as often as the imagery soared. But Pandora is still a good place to park yourself for three hours.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Is it a great movie?  Maybe not.  But it is a great step forward in moviemaking.  Shrug it off if that makes you feel better, but starting today you live in a post-Avatar movie world.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: Vast, beautiful and meticulously detailed.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Is it or is it not stupendously friggin' rad? And the answer is yes. For most of the first hour, a good portion of the second, and even many of the 40 minutes left after that, Avatar is stupendously friggin' rad.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: It is a very expensive-looking, very flashy entertainment, albeit one that groans under the weight of clumsy storytelling in the second half and features some of the most godawful dialogue this side of "Attack of the Clones."
At the end of a decade defined by much bellyaching about "the death of cinema" (including, on occasion, by this critic), Avatar concludes, appropriately enough, with an image of rebirth.

author: David Edelstein
quote: Pantheism, Cameronism: In Avatar, what's the diff? Now he's king of a world he made from scratch.

author: Peter Bradshaw
quote: The sheer laborious silliness of Avatar feels like harder work the second time around and its essential problem is more prominent. [2022 re-release]

author: Bilge Ebiri
quote: Avatar may be derivative, but it’s not insincere. Cameron clearly feels every beat of the story along with his viewer. He lets us discover Pandora through Jake Sully’s (Sam Worthington) eyes, first as a fearsome, terrifying place, then as a land of unimaginable awe and delight. [2022 re-release]

author: Marco Vito Oddo
quote: Cameron’s epic can still thrill the audience with breathtaking set pieces, bring them to tears with moving moments, and amaze people willing to explore a fantasy land like no other. Every frame is developed with such care that the movie remains one of the most beautiful works of art ever created by Hollywood. [2022 re-release]

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Grounded in the direct, disarming truth of their experience, the movie has a straightforward lack of cheap sentiment that saves it from being either too maudlin or saccharine-sweet.

author: Michael Rechtshaffen
quote: Bullock is an irrepressible hoot in writer-director John Lee Hancock's otherwise thoroughly conventional take on Michael Lewis' fact-based book "The Blind Side: Evolution of a Game."

author: Joe Leydon
quote: Uplifting and entertaining feel-good, fact-based sports drama.

author: Melissa Anderson
quote: Blind Side the movie peddles the most insidious kind of racism, one in which whiteys are virtuous saviors, coming to the rescue of African-Americans who become superfluous in narratives that are supposed to be about them.

author: David Fear
quote: It’s just blinkered middle-class pandering at its most shameless.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: A feel-good movie that never stops feeling good. The film is based on a true story (it was adapted from a nonfiction best-seller by Michael Lewis), but you never feel that Hancock has honestly captured what's true about it.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Michael as a character is defined almost solely by his helplessness and gratitude. He's as lovable as a lost puppy, but a more perceptive movie than The Blind Side would have let us see him from another angle.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: In a head-to-head comparison, one would be hard-pressed not to declare that "Precious" is the better film - it makes fewer compromises and doesn't shy from showing the true ugliness only hinted at in this movie, but The Blind Side is more accessible. It's easier to digest. In the end, both films tell stories of triumph over adversity - a category of drama that uplifts while offering a dollop of social commentary.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: It’s not an altogether convincing portrait, but it is an entertaining, even moving one, and the forcefulness of Bullock's presence goes a long way in pulling the film back from the brink of cuddliness.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: The story is inspiring and involves sports, but to call it an inspirational sports story would be wrong; its real center is Leigh Anne Tuohy (Sandra Bullock in a fine performance), the strong-willed woman whose love and generosity helped turn a mute, hopeless boy with no social or academic skills into a functioning young man with a promising future.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Veers perilously close to the concept of poverty tourism.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: Sports movies have a long, troubled history of well-meaning white paternalism, with poor black athletes finding success through white charity. But The Blind Side, based on Michael Lewis’ non-fiction book, finds a new low.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: Bullock’s levelheaded acting frequently saves the movie from emotional garishness.

author: David Hiltbrand
quote: An engaging if transparent tearjerker of the first water.

author: Marc Mohan
quote: A facile, feel-good fable that substitutes cliché for reality at nearly every turn.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: Unlike the tale told in "Precious", however, The Blind Side's story is contrived, storybook sweet, credulity-straining and ... um, true.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Never dull, but it's rarely more than gently entertaining.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: Wisely, Hancock has given the film as much humor as heart.

author: Stephen Cole
quote: A football story that deserves a penalty flag every other play for piling on the sentiment.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Has strong performances and stirring football scenes.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: The film, not unsurprisingly for a holiday- (and football-) season release from a major Hollywood studio, plays this story straight down the middle, shedding nuance and complication in favor of maximum uplift.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: It's fair to say that Bullock's appealing portrait of a strong-willed Tennessee belle ranks among the best work of her career. It's just too bad the movie around her comes up short.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: What makes The Blind Side a Thanksgiving treat is director Hancock's subtle touch and admirable refusal to yield to sports movie clichés, something he did previously with "The Rookie" and "Remember the Titans."

author: Mike Scott
quote: One of the reasons it's so effective is because it's based on a real-life, odds-defying story: that of mountainous Baltimore Ravens offensive tackle Michael Oher (played by Quinton Aaron).

author: Michael Sragow
quote: Without restraint or subtlety, but with a lot of heart and energy, this movie tells a real-life tall tale.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: For all The Blind Side's flaws, it's impossible not to get caught up in Michael Oher's life.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Best performance, minute for minute, comes from Adriane Lenox, whose cameo as Michael's drug-addled mother is the film's standout.

author: Elias Savada
quote: This may be Bullock's best performance. Ever.

author: Ian Freer
quote: Bullock delivers a towering performance that grabs the movie and the Oscar race by the scruff of the neck. You will be moved, but at the price of any nuance or complexity.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: No true fan of science fiction -- or, for that matter, cinema -- can help but thrill to the action, high stakes and suspense built around a very original chase movie.

author: Justin Chang
quote: Though compelling throughout, District 9 never becomes outright terrifying, largely because Blomkamp is less interested in exploiting his aliens for cheap scares than in holding up a mirror to our own bloodthirsty, xenophobic species.

author: Scott Foundas
quote: District 9 whizzes by with a resourcefulness and mordant wit nearly worthy of its obvious influences: "Invasion of the Body Snatchers," "Dawn of the Dead," and "Starship Troopers."

author: Matthew Sorrento
quote: The humanity of District 9 adds another dimension to this multilayered, rewarding work -- one of the best of the summer, and undoubtedly the most inventive from the multiplex this year.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Madly original, cheekily political, altogether exciting District 9.

author: Joe Williams
quote: The reason District 9 reverberates so loudly is because its moral indignation is cranked to 11.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: Blomkamp and his entire cast and crew have created an instant genre classic that transcends the self-limiting ghetto implied by the term "science fiction" and instead, like precursors such as Robert Wise's "The Day the Earth Stood Still," engages not only the mind but the heart as well. It's magnificent.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Despite its creativity, the movie remains space opera and avoids the higher realms of science-fiction.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Anyone who watches District 9 and doesn't think of Apartheid, Nazis, and Josef Mengele needs to spend some time reading a few history books.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: South African director Neill Blomkamp set and shot the film around his native Johannesburg, so parallels to apartheid leap to mind. Yet the script he wrote with Terri Tatchell applies to any culture that bluntly excludes another.

author: Michael Sragow
quote: It's a bad joke that District 9 will be hailed for its "originality."

author: Scott Tobias
quote: District 9 fuses science fiction mayhem and biting social commentary as well as any film since "Starship Troopers." It’s the rare alien invasion story that has the aliens running scared.

author: Steven Rea
quote: There is a lot of shield-your-eyes ickiness in District 9, a lot of violence and gore. What there is not a lot of, however, is humanity - even in the film's depiction of the inhumanity humans are capable of.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: District 9 is very smart sci-fi, but that's just the beginning; it's also a scathing social satire hidden inside a terrific action thriller teeming with gross aliens and regrettable inter-species conflict. And it's a blast. . . .

author: Ty Burr
quote: The last time I felt the sort of outrageously kinetic action-movie high District 9 delivers, it was 1981 and George Miller, Mel Gibson, and "Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior" had just come roaring out of Australia.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Attention all geeks (and geeks at heart): Get ready for two hours of serious awesome.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Some movies pack such a terrific central idea, even their flaws can’t stop the train. District 9 is one of them.

author: Peter Travers
quote: This baby has the stuff to end the movie summer on a note of dazzle and distinction.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: District 9 proves that genre films, besides being a hell of a lot of fun, can say things you hadn't considered and show stuff you haven't seen.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Yes, the premise is delightful; no, the delight doesn't last.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: In the best B-movie tradition, the filmmakers embed their ideas in an ingenious, propulsive and suspenseful genre entertainment, one that respects your intelligence even as it makes your eyes pop (and, once in a while, your stomach turn).

author: Claudia Puig
quote: With its clever faux documentary style, this is the most imaginative science-fiction movie to come along in years.

author: Joanne Kaufman
quote: There's a wonderfully sly, farcical verve to these early moments, but it dissipates when the script, with its strains of "E.T." and "The Fly," moves into high sci-fi gear.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: This is a delicious premise, and Blomkamp, who first played with it in a 2005 short called "Alive in Joburg," has magnified and improved it with ferocious energy, wit and style.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: Every now and then, a film comes along that both defies and compels description. District 9 is one such movie: a science-fiction action vehicle so brilliantly and fully imagined that real life, when it resumes after the credits, arrives with a new sense of dread.

author: Jeannette Catsoulis
quote: Grabs you by the eyeballs from the very first frame.

author: Daniel Engber
quote: As an allegory of racial conflict and mass immigration, District 9 never really goes anywhere: The appealing premise fades into the background before 20 minutes have elapsed.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Unfortunately, as in many such big-screen comic books, the backstory beats the hell out of the present-tense plot.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: The movie falls into the same uneasy category as "Eight Legged Freaks": too tongue-in-cheek to be thrilling, not funny enough to be a comedy.

author: John Anderson
quote: A sci-fi-fueled indictment of man's inhumanity to man -- and the non-human -- District 9 is all horribly familiar, and transfixing.

author: Mike Scott
quote: To his credit, however, the often-playful Blomkamp never bludgeons his audience with any specific message. He's too busy letting 'er rip with his edge-of-your-seat, and unapologetically violent, sci-fi adventure.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: A memorable, monstrous fable that's consistently gripping.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: The ad campaign for the sci-fi thriller District 9, with mysterious billboards touting aliens among us, is highly creative and amusing. So, in patches, is the movie, which is a thinking man's, or man-boy's, "Transformers."

author: Staff (Not credited)
quote: A great-looking and smart film. It has enough action, wonder, depth, and action to keep any fan of the genre happy. The sociological undertones here are fascinating as well.

author: Chris Hewitt (1)
quote: Blomkamp’s prawn cocktail has more than enough stylistic chutzpah and originality to make District 9 an essential date.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: You don't feel bamboozled, fooled, or patronized by District 9, as you did by most of the summer blockbusters.  You feel winded, and shaken, and shamed.  [September 14, 2009, pg.115]

author: James Greenberg
quote: Topped by a fine cast, a first-rate script by Nick Hornby and tight direction by Lone Scherfig, the film is a smart, moving but not inaccessible entry in the coming-of-age canon.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Carey Mulligan shines in a captivating performance.

author: David Edelstein
quote: For all its original touches, though, An Education follows a conventional trajectory.

author: Scott Foundas
quote: Something of a deceptively packaged Oscar-season bonbon--a seemingly benign, classily directed year-I-became-a-woman nostalgia trip that conceals a surprisingly tart, morally ambiguous center.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: Lone Scherfig directs it all as if it were a breezy lark, so a third-act tonal shift makes for an incongruous, excessively moralistic fit with everything that’s preceded. Most insulting, though, is the way in which the climactic passages miraculously tidy up every frayed edge of Jenny’s life.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Afterward, you'll want to listen to the Beatles sing ''She's Leaving Home.'' It might be a girl like Jenny the lads had in mind.

author: Cliff Doerksen
quote: This British drama is handsomely textured and beautifully acted, though the script often feels giddily out of touch with the essential creepiness of the scenario.

author: Nathan Rabin
quote: An Education shares with Hornby’s best work trenchant insight into the way smart, hyper-verbal young people let the music, films, books, and art they love define themselves as they figure out who they are and what they want to be.

author: Peter Travers
quote: An Education is remarkable for the traps it doesn't fall into. Jenny, for all her naive impulses, isn't a victim.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: An Education captures the very limited possibilities for female liberation in early-'60s London -- with massive social change on the distant horizon, but not here yet -- in exquisite detail.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: This tale of an English schoolgirl's hard-won wisdom is thrilling --for the radiance of Carey Mulligan's Jenny, who's wonderfully smart and perilously tender; for the grace of Lone Scherfig's direction, and the brilliance of Nick Hornby's screenplay.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: This is a performance, and a film, to cherish for this year and always.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: As she's being put through her Oxford-prep paces, Jenny complains about "ticking off boxes," and at times, this film seems to be doing just that: coming-of-age drama, check. Youthful illusions shattered, check. But as with first love, so with the movies: The right girl makes it all worthwhile.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: If in hindsight An Education might make you a little queasy, it is hard to resist, like David himself.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Through stellar performances, clever writing and exquisite cinematography, the story is fresh and thoroughly captivating.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: Despite the lingering aroma of Victorian rot shrouding 1961, An Education is excitingly young.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Watch Mulligan's face as she goes from weary to awakened, and see it all come together.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Bracingly perceptive about the human comedy.

author: David Denby
quote: An Education is perceptive and entertaining, but it doesn’t have the jolting vitality of, say, “Notes on a Scandal,” which dramatized an even more unconventional liaison--older woman, fifteen-year-old boy.

author: Ty Burr
quote: Best of all, An Education isn’t alarmist. It knows other people can’t seduce us if we don’t seduce ourselves first and that Jenny is level-headed enough to handle it and learn.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Hornby's humane and humorous screenplay is true to the film's title: In short order, young Jenny finds out important truths about identity, glamour and how adults really think and live.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: In the end, this is more a character study of Jenny than a tale of tortured love, and a reminder that any education worth having comes with its share of trauma.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: This happens in 1961, when 16-year-old girls were a great deal less knowing than they are now. Yet the movie isn't shabby or painful, but romantic and wonderfully entertaining.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The film version of “Breakfast at Tiffany’s” came out in the year in which An Education is set, and beyond the hairstyles, there’s something of the willful, gleeful Golightly reinvention expert about Jenny.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Hornby is a fine craftsman and his dialogue sparkles, though occasionally the scenes are too calculated.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Disarming and unexpectedly poignant, An Education contrasts the knowledge learned in school with that learned from life.

author: Dan Jolin
quote: A decent but unremarkable film with a big, unforgettable central performance. Carey Mulligan passes with First-Class Honours.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: The film wouldn't work at all, though, if Sarsgaard didn't strike the perfect balance between snaky predator and love-struck fool.

author: Staff (Not credited)
quote: A beguiling little film that, with deceptive restraint and forthrightness, opens up worlds of roiling, contradictory emotions.

author: Elias Savada
quote: Technical elements are among the best this year. Photography, editing, music, production design, and costumes all add seamless period flavor to the puritanical stew that was London almost a half-century ago.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It is, in its quiet, precise, classical way, nearly perfect.

author: Joe Williams
quote: The combination of a literate script, an adroit cast and an economical style is simple addition that achieves an alchemical feat: the best film of the year.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: A distinctive story with universal appeal.

author: Mike Scott
quote: It's a career-making performance that relies as much on charm as on acting ability -- and Mulligan has both.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: The result is an intense, action-driven war pic, a muscular, efficient standout that  simultaneously conveys the feeling of combat from within as well as what it looks like on the ground.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: A near-perfect movie about men in war, men at work. Through sturdy imagery and violent action, it says that even Hell needs heroes.

author: Derek Elley
quote: Boal's script stirs a little of everything into the pot, which boils down into seven setpieces divided by brief intervals of camaraderie/conflict among the three protags.

author: David Edelstein
quote: The Hurt Locker might be the first Iraq-set film to break through to a mass audience because it doesn't lead with the paralysis of the guilt-ridden Yank. The horror is there, but under the rush.

author: Deborah Young
quote: Tensely action-packed and muscularly directed by Kathryn Bigelow, this tale of an elite U.S. army bomb disposal unit in Baghdad is a familiar story in new clothes, targeted at the young male demographic.

author: David Denby
quote: A small classic of tension, bravery, and fear, which will be studied twenty years from now when people want to understand something of what happened to American soldiers in Iraq. If there are moviegoers who are exhausted by the current fashion for relentless fantasy violence, this is the convincingly blunt and forceful movie for them.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: There's something about this story, and this war, that brings out the stripped-down conceptual artist in her (Bigelow): Against blank canvases of desert sand and rubble, explosive wires are linked to nerve ends, and everything that matters depends on the twitch of a muscle or a finger on a button.

author: Scott Foundas
quote: A full-throttle body shock of a movie. It gets inside you like a virus, puts your nerves in a blender, and twists your guts into a Gordian knot.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: This is a tense, well-crafted motion picture that keeps viewers on edge. It's an exhausting 130 minutes; many viewers will leave the theater feeling drained.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Here's the Iraq War movie for those who don't like Iraq War movies.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: For the first hour or more, The Hurt Locker boldly forsakes any conventional narrative hook beyond the ongoing tensions between these men and the terrifying grind of defusing bombs day after day.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: The best nondocumentary American feature made yet about the war in Iraq.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: After The Hurt Locker (which is without question the most exciting and least ideological movie yet made about the war in Iraq), everyone will remember Renner's name.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Both a psychological portrait and an exciting action film.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: A first-rate action thriller, a vivid evocation of urban warfare in Iraq, a penetrating study of heroism and a showcase for austere technique, terse writing and a trio of brilliant performances. Most of all, though, it’s an instant classic that demonstrates, in a brutally hot and dusty laboratory setting, how the drug of war hooks its victims and why they can’t kick the habit.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The drawback is that even though The Hurt Locker is extremely effective in places, it ultimately feels unformed and somewhat unfinished.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: The adrenaline rush of war has been largely missing from Hollywood's Iraq, but it's certainly front and center in The Hurt Locker, the first war movie in a while that feels as if it could have starred John Wayne.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Overwhelmingly tense, overflowing with crackling verisimilitude, it's both the film about the war in Iraq that we've been waiting for and the kind of unqualified triumph that's been long expected from director Kathryn Bigelow.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: Stretched both timewise and for plausibility.

author: Staff (Not credited)
quote: What you'll remember most will be Renner's remarkably complex commander. By the time we finally figure him out, it's become clear we've witnessed a star-making performance, in a movie that deserves to stand as one of the defining films of the decade.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Renner gives a full-bore performance of great individuality and industriousness, but essentially his character is as glamorized as any classic Westerner.

author: Nick Antosca
quote: Kathryn Bigelow's The Hurt Locker is a grinding, nightmarish machine.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: A great film, an intelligent film, a film shot clearly so that we know exactly who everybody is and where they are and what they’re doing and why.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Vivid, assured and extremely suspenseful.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: The tension is enough to make you slightly sick, and the overall mood of the thing is deeply dispiriting, but then, nobody ever said that war isn't hell.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: In The Hurt Locker, the thrill is unexpectedly contagious. You don't realize how riveted you are until you're back on American soil observing James in civilian life.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Fused with paranoia and almost unbearable suspense, The Hurt Locker is powerful stuff.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: This one enters the pantheon of great American war films.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: When viewers are ultimately released from The Hurt Locker's exhilarating vice grip, they'll find themselves shaken, energized and, more than likely, eager to see it again.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Episodic and, at times, overwrought. And occasionally its deliberate opacity becomes too cloudy. But the things that shine through are remarkable. War is indeed Hell, it tells us, but that isn't necessarily a bad thing if you're filled with demons.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Such is the extraordinary achievement of The Hurt Locker: it has the perspective of years when those years have yet to pass.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Like every war before it, the U.S. invasion of Iraq has generated its share of movies. But The Hurt Locker is the first of them that can properly be called a masterpiece.

author: Michael Sragow
quote: The Hurt Locker redefines war-film electricity.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: At once an unforgettable war film and a brilliant character study.

author: Ian Nathan
quote: The most literally exciting film you will see this year. Forget the off-putting banner of another Iraq movie -- go, watch, marvel, endure and book in the palliative of a stiff drink afterwards.

author: Peter Bradshaw
quote: For my money, Bigelow says more about the agony and tragedy of war than all those earnest, well-meaning movies that sound as if they've been co-scripted by Josh and Toby from The West Wing.

author: Gabe Toro
quote: It shows a concern for spatial discrepancies, between characters, between action and intention, between life and death. It’s one of many reasons why The Hurt Locker is one of the most exciting movies you’ll see this year.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: The film is by no means terrible -- its two hours and 32 minutes running time races by -- but those things we think of as being Tarantino-esque, the long stretches of wickedly funny dialogue, the humor in the violence and outsized characters strutting across the screen, are largely missing.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: A violent fairy tale, an increasingly entertaining fantasia in which the history of World War II is wildly reimagined so that the cinema can play the decisive role in destroying the Third Reich.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: In Tarantino's besotted historical reverie, real-life villains Adolf Hitler and Joseph Goebbels are played as grotesque jokes. The Basterds are played as exaggeratedly tough Jews. The women are femmes fatales.?

author: Nick Antosca
quote: The biggest, weirdest, ugliest cartoon in Inglourious Basterds is Aldo Raine, the Nazi-killing American Lieutenant played by Brad Pitt.

author: Chris Hewitt (1)
quote: With a confidence typical of its director, the last line of Inglourious Basterds is, "This might just be my masterpiece." While that may not be true, this is an often dazzling movie that sees QT back on exhilarating form.

author: David Edelstein
quote: Even more than his other genre mash-ups, this is a switchback journey through Tarantino’s twisted inner landscape, where cinema and history, misogyny and feminism, sadism and romanticism collide and split and re-bond in bizarre new hybrids. The movie is an ungainly pastiche, yet on some wacked-out Jungian level it’s all of a piece.

author: David Denby
quote: Inglourious Basterds is not boring, but it’s ridiculous and appallingly insensitive.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: Energetic, inventive, swaggering fun, Quentin Tarantino's Inglourious Basterds is a consummate Hollywood entertainment--rich in fantasy and blithely amoral.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: With Inglourious Basterds, Quentin Tarantino has made his best movie since "Pulp Fiction." He has also made what could arguably be considered the most audacious World War II movie of all-time.

author: Joe Williams
quote: With its exploded notions of heroism, torture-rack dramatics and kamikaze gusto, it's a fiendishly entertaining flick.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: For all its stylistic flourishes and interlocking storylines, Inglourious Basterds is, at its bullet-riddled core, a bloody good war movie, twisting and twisted and full of wordy shrapnel but no less kickass for it.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: A big, bold, audacious war movie that will annoy some, startle others and demonstrate once again that he’s (Tarantino) the real thing, a director of quixotic delights.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Basterds isn't so revolutionary or so finely crafted as "Pulp Fiction" was, but it crackles with the same energy and imagination and chutzpah.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The result is a Jewish “Death Wish,” to borrow Pauline Kael’s description of “Marathon Man,” amped up to epoch-changing proportions, made by a gentile writer-director with an unlimited appetite for celluloid, right down to its highly flammable properties.

author: Michael Sragow
quote: The only hope for Inglourious Basterds is that audiences will embrace it the way the Broadway crowd did "Springtime for Hitler": because it's so bad they think it's good.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Tarantino has already caught some flack for daring to use the Holocaust as material for another of his bloody live-action cartoons, but of course the generation that experienced it for real has mostly faded away. In that sense Inglourious Basterds is a social marker as startling as "Easy Rider" was in its day.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: A fairy tale about the infinite power of film, it boasts all his swaggering trademarks: rapid-fire dialogue, gleeful violence, endless cultural references. But it's the sharp-eyed deliberation that makes the greatest impact.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: May be the most fun you'll have at the movies this summer.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: Spelling may not be Quentin Tarantino’s forte, but his grasp of language (both verbal and visual) is peerless.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Less a Holocaust retribution fantasy than a messy homage to war movies, and to movies, period.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: its moments of greatness--and there are more than a couple--feel weirdly disconnected, stuck in a movie that doesn’t know how to put them together, or find a good way to move from one to the next.

author: Ty Burr
quote: A manically playful revenge fantasia made from the spare parts of Sergio Leone spaghetti westerns and strapping World War II action flicks.

author: Peter Travers
quote: For anyone professing true movie love, there's no resisting it.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: It's just possible that Tarantino, having played a trick on history, is also fooling his fans. They think they're in for a Hollywood-style war movie starring Brad Pitt. What they're really getting is the cagiest, craziest, grandest European film of the year.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Let's start with this certainty: No one but Quentin Tarantino could possibly have made Inglourious Basterds . Now add another: No one but his most ardent fans will be entirely glad that Quentin Tarantino did make Inglourious Basterds .

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Clocking in at 2 hours and 32 minutes, it is unforgivably leisurely, almost glacial, a film that loses its way in the thickets of alternative history and manages to be violent without the start-to-finish energy that violence on screen usually guarantees.

author: Joanne Kaufman
quote: Nothing about the emotionally unmoored Inglourious Basterds adds up. Whether it's parody, farce or a fever dream is anyone's guess.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Mostly, though, there is Landa, whose unctuous charm, beautifully modulated by Mr. Waltz, gives this unwieldy, dragging movie a much-needed periodic jolt.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Tarantino exercises both his obsession with vengeance and his fascination with the movies.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It's a film with many strengths, but it's not a knockout. And that's Tarantino's own fault, though not in the first way you might imagine.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: It's not enough to say that Inglourious Basterds is Quentin Tarantino's best movie. It's the first movie of his artistic maturity, the film his talent has been promising for more than 15 years.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Unwieldy, long-winded, self-indulgently nutso and, in places, very, very boring. It also caps off its two-and-a-half-hour run time with an extended finale – partially orchestrated to David Bowie's "Cat People" theme song, no less – that I could watch again and again with pleasure

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Tarantino's radical rewriting of the war's ending is audacious and perversely enthralling. But if Inglorious Basterds were about something more than the cinematic thrill of watching Nazis suffer, it could have been a revelation.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Isn't about history or war, or people and their problems, or anything of substance or meaning. It's a movie about other movies. For all its visual bravura and occasional bursts of antic inspiration, it feels trivial, the work of a kid who can't stop grabbing his favorite shiny plaything.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: The film on the whole feels unusually labored and conventional by Tarantino standards. Reducing World War II to juvenilia isn't the problem; the problem is that juvenilia needs to pop.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: No one else in Inglourious Basterds comes close to Landa for sheer charisma.

author: Nick Antosca
quote: Shot in the manner of a grueling horror picture, with jittery edits to half-remembered traumas and glistening close-ups on the faces of monsters.

author: Duane Byrge
quote: As Precious, Sidibe is superb, allowing us to see the inner warmth and beauty of a young woman who, to her world's cruel eyes, might seem monstrous.

author: John Anderson
quote: An urban nightmare with a surfeit of soul, Precious: Based on the Novel 'Push' by Sapphire is like a diamond -- clear, bright, but oh so hard.

author: David Edelstein
quote: The elements of Precious are powerful and shocking, but the movie is programmed. It is its own study guide.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: What we have here is a fouled-up fairy tale of oppression and empowerment, and it’s hard not to be ensnared by its mixture of rank maleficence and easy reverie. The gap between being genuinely stirred and having your arm twisted, however, is narrower than we care to admit.

author: Scott Foundas
quote: Yet even when the movie is at its most schizoid, Precious still packs a wallop.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: Given the months-long hype, what’s most bewildering about Sundance sensation Precious is its overall shrug-worthiness.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Manages the task of being both heartbreaking and heart-warming.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: It's a potent and moving experience, because by the end you feel you've witnessed nothing less than the birth of a soul.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: A great American film.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The performances are so plainspoken and direct that they manage to push the material beyond the confines of a mere social-problem tract -- as played by the cast, these characters aren't symbols of inner-city hardship, but people.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: This drama about an obese, illiterate black teen in Harlem practically guarantees some emotional uplift. But when it arrives, eventually, its authority is unimpeachable, so deeply has director Lee Daniels (Monster's Ball) immersed us in the depths of human ugliness.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: This is an exceptional film about nearly unendurable circumstances, endured. You will come out the other side of it a markedly enriched filmgoer.

author: Noel Murray
quote: Not even Douglas Sirk or Lars von Trier would heap so much abuse on a heroine. And yet, on its own melodramatic, tear-jerking terms, Precious works.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Claireece "Precious" Jones, played by Gabourey Sidibe, 24, in an astounding debut that brims with grit and amazing grace.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Genuinely and irresistibly inspirational.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: Nimble and self-assured as Mr. Daniels’s direction may be, he could not make you believe in “Precious” unless you were able to believe in Precious herself. You will.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: Its story ends up packing an emotional wallop as substantial as its title character.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: Nothing quite prepares you for the rough-cut diamond that is Precious. A rare blend of pure entertainment and dark social commentary, this shockingly raw, surprisingly irreverent and absolutely unforgettable story.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Despite its melodramatic moments, remarkable performances drive home the film's inspiring message.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: In its eagerness to drag us through the lower depths of human experience, Precious leaves no space for the audience to breathe or to draw our own conclusions. For a film about empowerment and self-actualization, it wields an awfully large cudgel.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: The film's real strength is its cast, from an Oscar-bound Mo'Nique to a notably deglammed Mariah Carey.

author: V.A. Musetto
quote: To its credit, this remarkable film does not contrive a happy ending. Under the circumstances, even a mildly hopeful one seems like a triumph of the highest order.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: It melodramatizes everything and yet its overall effect is something more than melodrama.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: You go to a Daniels movie not to be entertained, but edified. While not everyone goes to the movies for self-improvement, you will leave this one having witnessed phenomenal acting.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: A movie about an obese Harlem teenager who's raped by her father and abused by her mother. It's depressing, devastating, harrowing and repulsive. But there are lyric flights of hope interspersed among that raw naturalism, and that's what makes this movie amazing.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Qualifies as the most painful, poetic and improbably beautiful film of the year.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Although Precious is based on a novel, it's an act of truth-telling on behalf of a character in hellish enslavement.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: This bruising, harrowing movie would be impossible to sit through without at least a hint of light at the end of its astonishingly dark tunnel.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: This movie catalogs a wealth of human ugliness. It’s even been made to look ugly, presumably to underscore the horror movie that is Precious’s life.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Precious can’t be endorsed as entertainment:  the circumstances and incidents and emotions in the film are far too dark and painful.  But there is exhilaration in its daring, in its craft and in the powerhouse work of its principal actresses.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: It is certainly the best button-pushing movie of the year.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Precious is a bit like having a piano dropped on your head: messy but memorable.

author: Michael Sragow
quote: It's cathartic and exhilarating.

author: Mike Scott
quote: Precious is painful, it is harrowing, it is emotionally exhausting. It is also a singular film, one that is as difficult to compare to another as it is to forget.

author: Angie Errigo
quote: While it may not be perfect on a technical level, dramatically it’s a blow-your-socks-off triumph. Be moved. Very, very moved.

author: David Edelstein
quote: A Serious Man is not only hauntingly original, it’s the final piece of the puzzle that is the Coens. Combine suburban alienation, philosophical inquiry, moral seriousness, a mixture of respect for and utter indifference to Torah, and, finally, a ton of dope, and you get one of the most remarkable oeuvres in modern film.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: The always surprising Coen brothers have finally made a very serious movie with A Serious Man. It's about God, man's place in the world and the meaning of life, so naturally it's one of their funnier movies.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: One doesn't know how (auto)biographical any or all of this is, but there's a tartness to the telling of what amounts to a well-shaped series of anecdotes that bespeaks distant pain or, at least, wincing memory twisted into mordant comedy by time and sensibility.

author: David Denby
quote: A Serious Man, like “Burn After Reading,” is in their bleak, black, belittling mode, and it’s hell to sit through.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: As usual, though, the Coens have more venal satisfactions in mind. "The fun of the story for us," they crow in the notes for this loathsome movie, "was inventing new ways to torture Larry."

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: See this film immediately.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Working with affectionate mockery, the Coens take the cinder-block-synagogue banality of American Jewish life in 1967 and make it look as archly exotic as the loopy Scandinavian-American winterscape of "Fargo."

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The presence of so many low-key performers gives A Serious Man a very different, distinctly non-Hollywood vibe. The absence of familiar faces allows the Coens to fully immerse their audience in the time (1967) and place (the U.S. Midwest) of the story.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: I found this beautifully crafted movie to be frequently hilarious, consistently surprising and rigged with spring-loaded narrative bombs, from its opening scene to its devastating final shot.

author: Noel Murray
quote: WQholly a Coen brothers movie, in that it’s full of exaggerated characters and comic cruelty, anchored to a way of looking at the world that seems to posit a fundamental absence of meaning. And yet there’s something sweet and even a little heartening about the movie, too.

author: Peter Travers
quote: This seriously funny movie, artfully photographed by the great Roger Deakins, is spiritual in nature, barbed in tone, and, oh, yeah, it stings like hell.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: To absorb God's body blows, this disquieting, haunting movie says, is to be fully alive. To do otherwise could kill you.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Writer-directors Joel and Ethan have seized the opportunity afforded by the Oscar-winning success of "No Country for Old Men," to make their most personal, most intensely Jewish film, a pitch-perfect comedy of despair that, against some odds, turns out to be one of their most universal as well.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: The story is at once hilarious and horrific, its significance both self-evident and opaque. The same could be said of most of the Coen brothers’ movies, in which human existence and the attempt to find meaning in it are equally futile, if also sometimes a lot of fun. (For us, at least.)

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: What do the Coen brothers want of us? More specifically, what do they want us to think of the repellent people in this pitilessly bleak movie?

author: Dana Stevens
quote: A Serious Man is an exquisitely realized work; the filmmakers' technical mastery of their craft, always impressive, has become absolute. The script reads like a novel, densely allusive, funny, and terse.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: A wonderfully odd, bleakly comic and thoroughly engrossing film.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: The humor is sharp and so are the judgments, which pile on until the characters are nearly suffocated under the weight of so much disdain.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: May not have the starry casts of the Coens' more recent films, but it has plenty of heart and soul.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Have I mentioned A Serious Man is so rich and funny? This isn't a laugh-laugh movie, but a wince-wince movie. Those can be funny too.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: A tart, brilliantly acted fable of life’s little cosmic difficulties, a Coen brothers comedy with a darker philosophical outlook than “No Country for Old Men” but with a script rich in verbal wit.

author: Ty Burr
quote: It’s a work of cruel comic genius, in some ways even crueler than “No Country for Old Men.’’

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: The movie is funny, definitely funny. But underlying the humor is a vision so bleak, so despairing and so utterly hopeless as to make "No Country for Old Men" almost look cheerful.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It’s a story that begins in an ancient riddle and ends, perfectly, in the rumble of an oncoming storm.  It’s about life, A Serious Man is, and it’s as close, I think, as any American narrative movie of recent vintage has come to touching on the uncanniness of it.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: May not be "Fargo," but it nestles comfortably somewhere beneath that masterpiece and "Miller's Crossing," yet far above such forgettables as "The Ladykillers" and "Intolerable Cruelty."

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Like the Coens’ protagonist in "The Man Who Wasn’t There," Stuhlbarg is driven to an existential crisis, but in contrast to the earlier movie, with its tired noir moves, this one is earnestly engaged in the question of what constitutes a life well lived.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: A comedy of discomfort -- and one of their (Coen brothers) best, most insightful and most provocative films.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: This unsettling, shaggy, surrealistic pillow of a movie - a mixed bag more funny-strange than ha-ha.

author: Rick Groen
quote: A seriously black comedy. Black, because affliction and angst abound. Comic, because this rampant bleakness is presented as nothing more than an amusing bauble.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: It is rich with ideas and contemplations and packed with the sort of existential jokes that tickle the Coen boys so.

author: Matthew Sorrento
quote: Once again, the Coens' tale of the damned is damn funny.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: That song (Jefferson Airplane's Somebody to Love), which becomes a sort of mantra to the movie, is the key to understanding what the Coens are after: When the truth is found to be lies, and all the joy within you dies, you better find somebody to love.

author: Michael Sragow
quote: All the Coens come up with is a movie about bad things happening to limited people.

author: Mike Scott
quote: The only thing missing from the film -- which is frequently amusing but too bleak to be consistently laugh-out-loud funny -- is a genuine connection with its audiences, or at least those audiences not raised in 1960s Jewish suburbia.

author: Dan Jolin
quote: Admirably low-key, deeply compelling and their warmest movie since Fargo.

author: Peter Bradshaw
quote: Euphoric, sad and thoughtful all at once, this strange and wonderful film is rounded off with a gloriously well-crafted apocalyptic vision and a chilling intimation of divine retribution for earthly wrongdoing. The Coens have finished the noughties as America's pre-eminent film-makers.

author: Andrew Pulver
quote: Despite the opaque story line, their film is a glittering, perfectly honed artifice; but what pushes it into the Coen premier league is the sense that, as with Fargo, there's something very personal going on here.
The Coens nod at some familiar stylistic tropes – florid swearing, sexual euphemism, crusty, aged characters – but the film’s potency is rooted in quiet precision and detailed realisation.

author: Michael Rechtshaffen
quote: Winsome, touching and arguably the funniest Pixar effort ever, the gorgeously rendered, high-flying adventure is a tidy 90-minute distillation of all the signature touches that came before it.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: A captivating odd-couple adventure that becomes funnier and more exciting as it flies along.

author: David Edelstein
quote: By all means, see Up in its 3-D incarnation: The cliff drops are vertiginous, and the scores of balloons--bunched into the shape of one giant balloon--are as pluckable as grapes.

author: Robert Wilonsky
quote: The first 10 minutes of Up are flawless; the final 80 minutes, close enough. (Though, note this: Do not see Up in 3-D. It's inessential to the tale and altogether distracting.)

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: A lovely, thoughtful, and yes, uplifting adventure.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Up is not as transcendent as last year's "WALL-E," and doesn't rank near the top of Pixar's pantheon of great features, but it's a solid (and in some ways innovative) fantasy adventure that mixes comedy, action, and drama into a satisfying whole.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Extending the patented Pixar mix of humor and heart, Up is the studio's most deeply emotional and affecting work.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: We will be comparing Up with classics like "The Wizard of Oz" for years to come.

author: Scott Foundas
quote: Up emerges as a gentle hymn to adventure of both the soaring, storybook variety and the smaller, less obvious kind -- the perilous, unpredictable and richly rewarding journey of ordinary, everyday life.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: This is another masterwork from Pixar, which is leading the charge in modern animation.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Rousing, exhilarating entertainment.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: While their latest achievement can't quite one-up "WALL-E," it offers soaring highs that are bound to enchant viewers of any age.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: The exhilarating film pays tribute to Buster Keaton's "The Balloonatic" by way of its slapstick, and to Hayao Miyazaki's "Howl's Moving Castle" by way of its watercolor palette and traveling domicile.

author: Tasha Robinson
quote: Up is challenging, emotionally and narratively, but it trusts viewers to keep up; Pixar has never been interested in talking down to children or their parents.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Rarely has any film, let alone an animated one powered by the logic of dream and fantasy, been able to move so successfully -- and so effortlessly -- through so many different kinds of cinematic territory.

author: Ty Burr
quote: On the most basic level the new film is pure vaudeville: a loopy flyaway fantasy that's hysterically funny if only to keep the darkness at bay.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Up is a breathtaking ride into the realm of pure imagination.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Passages of glorious imagination are invariably matched by stock characters and banal story choices.

author: Staff (Not credited)
quote: Each character has their own story, and Pixar never sacrifices their development just for a happy ending.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: I'm still left, though, with an unshakable sense of Up being rushed and sketchy, a collection of lovely storyboards that coalesced incompletely or not at all.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Easily the summer's, and probably the year's, most enchanting movie, Up is a buoyant delight.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Up is Pixar's most ambitious attempt yet to take animation to higher (and deeper) places than it's been before, and Giacchino's sprightly music keeps the whole thing, impossibly, aloft.

author: Kate Taylor
quote: Disney has historically peopled cartoons aimed at children with violent, gruesomely animated villains. For all its delicious whimsy, Up is no exception.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Some of the comic inventions are inspired: Muntz has a pack of dogs equipped with electronic voice boxes, which means they're talking dogs, only they speak as if they've learned English from a poorly translated Berlitz guide.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: The result is a soaring, touching, funny and altogether buoyant movie that lives up to its title in spirit and in form.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Save for a few inspired canine gags and a handful of very pretty visual details, Up left me cold. Its charms appear to have been applied with surgical precision; by the end, I felt expertly sutured, but not much else.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Is Up top-shelf Pixar? No. But is it quality summer movie entertainment? Absolutely. Even when the folks at Pixar aim to keep their feet solidly on the ground, they can't help but soar.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Has some great movie moments but also boring stretches.

author: Perry Seibert
quote: You get the feeling that, had Pixar been in business 25 years ago, Steven Spielberg might have made this movie for them as a follow-up to "Raiders of the Lost Ark."

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: An exquisite work of cinematic art that also happens to be the funniest, most touching, most exciting and most entertaining movie released so far this year.

author: Andrea Gronvall
quote: Writer-directors Pete Docter and Bob Peterson present hilarious insights into bird brains and canine psychology and treat thornier human emotions deftly.

author: Michael Sragow
quote: Everything about Up is an up, in the most visceral and poetic ways.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: As a piece of poetic compression, it ranks with the opening of Orson Welles's "The Magnificent Ambersons."

author: Matthew Sorrento
quote: After a strong takeoff, the film lands on dead grounds.

author: David Denby
quote: The movie is packed with lovely jokes, some of them funny in inexplicable ways.

author: Mike Scott
quote: A thoroughly uplifting bit of cinema.

author: Ian Freer
quote: If it had lived up to its golden first five minutes, Up would have been the film of the decade. As it is, it remains the best animated flick of 2009, a funny, moving, beautifully made argument that dreamers can move mountains.

author: Stephen Farber
quote: It's rare for a movie to be at once so biting and so moving. If Ryan's future seems bleak, there's something exhilarating about a movie made with such clear-eyed intelligence.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: The timing in the Clooney-Farmiga scenes is like splendid tennis, with each player surprising the other with shots but keeping the rally going to breathtaking duration.

author: David Edelstein
quote: Up in the Air is poised to be a smash, and Clooney--slim, dark, perfectly tailored--glamorizes insincerity in a way that makes you want to go out and lie.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: The film is a hybrid. Its backdrop is despair, but the foreground action has the silvery zest of a comedy.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: Up in the Air goes down like a sedative. This is a movie that's easy to like--and to dislike as well.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: Reitman, who also cowrote the screenplay, feels the constant need to "deepen" his characters, granting them wants and motivations--especially during the moralistic third act--that are totally alien to how they're initially portrayed.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Up in the Air is light and dark, hilarious and tragic, romantic and real. It's everything that Hollywood has forgotten how to do; we're blessed that Jason Reitman has remembered

author: Robert Wilonsky
quote: If Steven Soderbergh taught Clooney how to act in "Out of Sight," then Reitman has taught him how to stop acting. This is the most vulnerable, the most playful, the most human performance of his career.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Up in the Air takes the trust people once had in their jobs and pulls out the rug. It is a film for this time.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Reitman deserves credit for going through with a bitterly ironic ending, but the movie is marred by its warm condescension toward flyover country.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Farmiga has never been better than she is here. Rarely does she get to do comedy, and she and Clooney give Up in the Air's sustained air of engaging disengagement a heartbeat as well as a romantic charge.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Ryan may not be admirable, but Clooney makes him relatable. It's his deepest and nakedest performance.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Reitman brings the same mixture of comedy and drama to this movie that he brought to "Juno."

author: Wesley Morris
quote: At its best, Up in the Air invents new realms for old Hollywood sophistication.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Up in the Air is also optimistic about the perpetual themes that preoccupy so many movies that endure the test of time: Life is better with company. And everybody needs a co-pilot.

author: Peter Travers
quote: One-word reaction: bravo.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: What Up In The Air lacks in surprises--apart from an elusive final scene--it compensates for by conveying the pleasures of living from landing to landing, and the terror of floating away.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: One of the pleasures of Up in the Air is that its actresses share the frame with Mr. Clooney as equals, not props

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: The best of Up in the Air--meaning most of it--is right up there with the fresh and sophisticated comedies of Hollywood's golden age.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: Crisply funny and fleetly paced, it's in its quiet way one of the saddest things in the theaters all year.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Timeliness can be tricky to pull off convincingly in movies. It's tough to capture an era while it's still happening, yet Up in the Air does so brilliantly, with wit and humanity.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Full of clever one-liners, winning performances, and wistful indie music. It's impossible not to like it, which is precisely what's so annoying about it.

author: Rick Groen
quote: As for the implicit tragedy amidst the funny business, the swelling ranks of the unemployed, the movie has no solution but instead offers itself as implicit solace: Escape, ye wretches, into my clever humour and my nifty dialogue and my star's considerable charm.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: A smart, alert, supremely entertaining movie.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Comes off not as topical but as opportunist. The picture is brushed with a fine glaze of slickness, a product sealed in a blister pack. It's like airplane air -- it has a packaged freshness that isn't really fresh at all.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: Perhaps it's no surprise that Reitman has  come out with a lovely Hollywood romance that floats buoyantly along on a sea of sadness.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: One of the year's best films and so tapped into the zeitgeist that it's positively scary.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Up in the Air makes it look easy. Not just in its casual and apparently effortless excellence, but in its ability to blend entertainment and insight, comedy and poignancy, even drama and reality, things that are difficult by themselves but a whole lot harder in combination. This film does all that and never seems to break a sweat.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: This is a movie about, among other things, pain, and it's made by someone who understands its expression.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Reitman's blend of comedy and drama, romance and social observation make Up in the Air the ideal movie --- and maybe even a cure -- for the Great Recession blues.

author: David Ansen
quote: There hasn't been a studio movie as unapologetically adult, sophisticated, and nuanced as Up in the Air in some time.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Up in the Air may not end up as the best picture -- that will be decided by the Academy -- but it has landed in the middle of the discussion because it's laser-focused and right on time.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: For much of its going, Up in the Air moves with the same refreshing pace and attitude that marked Reitman's "Thank You for Smoking" and "Juno" -- with the added frisson that the subject matter is so torn-from-the-headlines that it feels, in a good way, like reality TV.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: The middle is terrific, especially in a lengthy, unassuming scene in which the three leads sit, sip drinks, and have a good chat: It marks one of the great celluloid pleasures of the year, so virtuosically written, performed, and filmed is it.

author: Ian Nathan
quote: This is smart, silky, sensitive, and funny old-school movie magic.

author: Michael Mariani
quote: A smart, brisk, but extremely thoughtful hybrid of star-vehicle and resonant depiction of right-now America.

author: Damon Wise
quote: A surprisingly fun, effervescent against-the-odds drama that offers an upbeat moral without the usual punishing survival-story clichés. Not for the faint-hearted, mind.

author: Mike Scott
quote: 127 Hours -- just like "Slumdog Millionaire" -- is a masterful slice of four-star cinema, featuring an irresistible performance by James Franco, breathtaking cinematography, and the kind of deep, searching soul that is absent from so much of what comes out of Hollywood.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: This is a deeply inspirational movie about the human spirit's refusal to give up, but it is also a portrait of a man too much in love with life to let go without a fight.

author: Steve Persall
quote: It's gory and gut-wrenching but strangely life-affirming.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: 127 Hours is based on Ralston's memoir, and it's a really good movie because director Danny Boyle is a genius.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: Danny Boyle's 127 Hours is the calm, cool, and tear-your-hair-out exciting mirror image of Tony Scott's bland and formulaic "Unstoppable."

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Before it traps Ralston, 127 Hours gives us ample evidence of his energy, zest and boyish charm and wit.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Although Ralston's act of desperation is admittedly difficult to watch, viewers who might avoid the film out of squeamishness would be depriving themselves of one of the year's most exhilarating cinematic experiences.

author: Ty Burr
quote: The scene appalls but doesn't offend; it's a "Worst-Case-Scenario Survival Handbook'' nightmare that resonates on the metaphysical level.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: 127 Hours never calms down. You suspect you're only getting half the truth of what this ordeal must've been like.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Shiver-making moments aside, in a important way 127 Hours suffers from the filmmaker's lack of nerve, a reluctance to let the audience taste Ralston's dread and the expectation of a slow, absurd death.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: 127 Hours, about an unimaginably unbearable experience, is pretty much an unbearable experience of its own. And yet, it must be said, it's exceptionally well made.

author: Noel Murray
quote: Aside from an exhilarating opening and a gruesome climax, the movie isn't all that rich emotionally; all the visual razzle-dazzle winds up serving a pat lesson about people needing other people.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Is the film watchable? Yes, compulsively.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Only a truly visionary filmmaker could take a story largely set in a cramped canyon and give it a sense of openness and hope.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: This is a survival manual turned into an existential prison-break movie; it cuts deep and, at its ecstatic climax, soars high.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: As a testament to positive thinking, 127 Hours will probably stand as a ringing affirmation for reckless survivalists. For those of us not so affirmed, Boyle's paean to heroism – a better title for it might have been "A Farewell to Arm" – is merely the best gross-out music video ever made.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: Even by my super-wimp standards, Aron's exit is surprisingly coy, coming from a filmmaker who gets his kicks from goosing the hell out of his audiences.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Like all great movies, 127 Hours takes us on a memorable journey. Which is not easy when 90 percent of the movie takes place with a virtually immobile hero in a very cramped setting.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Once Franco's on his own, everything is played across this terrific actor's deceptively goofy face.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Boyle's skill at wringing physical and emotional reactions from his audience is impressive; watching 127 Hours is, as intended, an experience of grueling intensity.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: But damned if Boyle, with the help of his star, doesn't make the experience almost… cheerful.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Like the A.R. Rahman score that drives the movie, the triumphant 127 Hours pays fitting tribute to Aron by being thrillingly alive.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: Mr. Boyle has a knack for tackling painful, violent or unpleasant subjects with unremitting verve and unstoppable joie de vivre.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: In the end, 127 Hours is one man's incredible, unforgettable journey; it took the extraordinary alchemy of Boyle and Franco to also make it ours.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: It's exciting, stirring, often funny, sometimes lyrical and unusually thoughtful. And, with that one egregious exception, genuinely pleasurable.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: Worse still, all that introspection adds up to a disappointingly shallow accumulation of regrets and life lessons, none of them surprising. After the adrenaline rush, 127 Hours turns to vapor.

author: Rex Reed
quote: For a story about a man who cannot move, the ordeal unfolds at a pace that keeps you breathless.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: This is a fascinating story of determination and survival that deserves to be told. It is ultimately uplifting but it's tough going to get to that point.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: Paradoxically, this is not a tale about summoning inner strength, but about shedding pride. Sometimes, there's no choice.

author: Dan Kois
quote: As Boyle's film flits from the real world-the heavy reality of a man in a canyon, pinned, near death-to the world of dreams and delusions, so Franco's performance transforms, encompassing both universes.

author: David Denby
quote: The movie is best when it calms down and concentrates on the sinister peculiarities of the experience, and when it focuses on Franco's face. [8 Nov. 2010, p . 93]

author: Pete Hammond
quote: With a tour-de-force performance from James Franco and an imaginative shooting style that relies on two cameras and inventive angles, what could have been static and deadly dull comes blazingly to life in this powerful and compelling story of one man's will to survive.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: A true-life adventure that turns into a one-man disaster movie - and the darker it gets, the more enthralling it becomes.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: It's a coming-of-age story - blunt, mythic, gut-wrenching.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: Franco is up to every bit of Boyle's challenge, capturing Aron's transition from clownish outdoorsman and party boy to an introspective chronicler of his own impending demise and a visionary lunatic.

author: Stephen Farber
quote: All of the key creative personnel contribute to the movie's nail-biting tension and unexpectedly moving finale. Jon Harris's editing is matchless, and Rahman's score effectively heightens the emotion. Ultimately, however, it is the talents of Boyle and Franco that sock this movie home.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: While Franco can sometimes be a wild card, getting increasingly self-conscious with recent roles, his take on Ralston feels both credible and compelling; few actors could have made us care so much, or disappeared so completely into the role.

author: Dan Jolin
quote: An extraordinary, intoxicating movie. Its hard, twisted edges may turn off some, but there's no faulting either Aronofsky's technical mastery or Portman's flawless performance.

author: Kristin Hunt
quote: Just like the final performance by its deeply disturbed heroine, Black Swan is perfect.

author: Mike Scott
quote: Arriving with a savage grace, director Darren Aronofsky's nightmare-come-to-life Black Swan cements his reputation not only as one of the more daring filmmakers of his generation, but also as an actor's director of the first order.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Like all his (Aronofsky) films, it's lurid, visually stimulating, thoughtful, absurd in spots, well-cast and unrelentingly intense.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Black Swan is a stage door melodrama putting new spins on cliches as old as "All About Eve" (and maybe Adam). Setting them among ballerinas as opposed to showgirls or movie stars doesn't make them any less familiar.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Artful, epic, operatic even, this thriller set in the world of ballet challenges the viewer with its intelligence and depth and wit.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: Black Swan is ridiculously over the top, but in a way that makes it fascinating to watch.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Whether your tastes are delicate or coarse, whether you prefer the ballet or horror movies, there is plenty in the film for you.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: It's all or nothing with Black Swan. Either you embrace its headlong descent into madness brought on by the pressures of artistic perfection, compounded by smothering anxiety, or you reject it. It's that simple.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: This drama-horror hybrid, set within a New York ballet company, strikes a tone more along the lines of the terrifying hallucinatories of Aronofsky's breakout film, "Requiem for a Dream," revisiting, too, favorite themes of monster mommies and female hysteria.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: Some girls fight over men. Ballerinas fight over parts. But the occasional brilliance of Black Swan is that it's a one-way fight. Nina battles herself.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: With Natalie Portman dominating the action and exhibiting a screen maturity not seen from her before, this all-stops-out Grand Guignol melodrama exhibits more than enough blood, sweat and tears (emphasis on the blood) to score nicely beyond the ballet crowd.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: The black/white duality isn't terribly interesting, but as in most of Aronofsky's films, an intense horror of the body and its uncontrollability fuels the rhapsodic psychodrama.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: A full-bore melodrama, told with passionate intensity, gloriously and darkly absurd. It centers on a performance by Natalie Portman that is nothing short of heroic.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Is Black Swan high-minded? I'm happy to say: No. It is extremely high-grade hokum, which is to say it offers several different and combustible varieties.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Lurid and voluptuous pulp fun, with a sensationalistic fairy-tale allure. You can't take it too seriously, but you can't tear your eyes away from it, either.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Not just any kind of trash, it's high-art trash, a kind of "When Tutu Goes Psycho" that so prizes hysteria over sanity that it's worth your life to tell when its characters are hallucinating and when they're not.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Aronofsky isn't loose enough, or canny enough, to be in touch with its camp soul.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: A psychosexual thriller that lures its viewers into a woozy nightmare.

author: David Edelstein
quote: This is, no doubt about it, a tour de force, a work that fully lives up to its director's ambitions.

author: Rex Reed
quote: This exercise in hysteria is so over the top that you don't know whether to scream or laugh. Despite an emotionally gripping performance by Natalie Portman, it's nothing more than a lavishly staged "Repulsion" in toe shoes.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Wild and woolly, the movie is a breathtaking head trip that hails from a long tradition of backstage melodramas: "42nd Street," "A Star Is Born," "All About Eve," and, yes, that kitschy '90s relic, "Showgirls."

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The look and feel of Black Swan, which captures the essence of a major New York ballet production, is one of Aronofsky's great successes.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Portman's portrait of an artist under siege is unmissable and unforgettable. So is the movie. You won't know what hit you.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Audaciously whacked-out and never less than entertaining, Darren Aronofsky's Black Swan mixes a backstage dance drama with a Freudian psychological thriller that's indebted to Roman Polanski's studies of shattered feminine psyches and David Cronenberg's movies about repressed bodies in rebellion.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: Indeed, White Swan/Black Swan dynamics almost work, but the horror-movie nonsense drags everything down the rabbit hole of preposterousness.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Black Swan is visceral and real even while it's one delirious, phantasmagoric freakout.

author: David Denby
quote: Off the dance floor, however, Black Swan is trashy and incoherent. Aronofsky, for all his gifts, is a gaudy maestro, opportunistic and insecure as an artist.

author: Keith Phipps
