quote: A florid, often lurid, completely enthralling film held in place by a disarming Portman, who rarely leaves the frame.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Me, I'm of two minds about a movie that wants to be a nail-ripping thriller and a statement on an artist's unholy communion with her role. It's reminiscent of older, better movies.

author: David Fear
quote: Both a baroque thriller set in New York's ballet demimonde and a portrait of artistry as schizoid perfectionism, Darren Aronofsky's new film percolates parallel lines of fine madness-but then, doubling down on duality is this movie's raison d'etre.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: To induce a state of dread and mesmerize with beauty is a rare, paradoxical achievement.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: A wicked, sexy and ultimately devastating study of a young dancer's all-consuming ambition, Black Swan serves as a fascinating complement to Darren Aronofsky's "The Wrestler," trading the grungy world of a broken-down fighter for the more upscale but no less brutal sphere of professional ballet.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: A near-irresistible exercise in bravura absurdity, Darren Aronofsky's Black Swan deserves to become a minor classic of heterosexual camp-at the very least, it's the most risible and riotous backstage movie since "Showgirls."

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Aronofsky blurs the line between reality and fantasy, turning the film into a gothic horror show that is fascinating and disappointing in equal measure. What's resplendently real, though, is the beauty of Ms. Portman's performance. She makes the whole lurid tale worthwhile.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: A love-it-or-hate-it movie. Put me in the (sort of) hate-it column. My slight qualification here is because Darren Aronofsky's movie starring Natalie Portman as an increasingly unhinged ballerina gets points for being unlike anything else that's out there.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: This eye-popping, inspired and often-demented (in a good way) cross between "The Red Shoes" and "All About Eve" channels horror maestros David Cronenberg, Brian De Palma and Dario Argento.

author: Jeannette Catsoulis
quote: For all its dazzling allure, Darren Aronofsky's Black Swan, a feverishly psycho thriller set in the hermetic world of classical ballet, proves a meaningless exercise in Grand Guignol exhibitionism.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: One of the best movies of the year.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: It's excessive and psychologically imprecise, coarse where it should be refined and too much like a David Cronenberg horror movie in places where restraint and intellectual rigor are called for.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Portman toils slavishly to realize Aronofsky's mad vision. It isn't her fault that, despite Black Swan's visual splendor and bursts of grand guignol excess, this emotionally inert movie never does grow wings.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: A near-masterpiece of a film set in the hothouse world of New York ballet.

author: Adam Smith
quote: An exhilarating fight-flick that, like its scrappy central character, is impossible not to root for.

author: David Ansen
quote: Urgent, gritty, sometimes weirdly funny, The Fighter might be considered his first feel-good movie. But Russell's too honest and acute an observer to serve up affirmation without leaving a subversive aftertaste of ambivalence and unease.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Virtually flawless performances and directorial execution render The Fighter one of the most thrilling movies of 2010.

author: Mike Scott
quote: A punch-drunk tale whose fitful ramble from Jerry Springer-style family seaminess to "Rocky"-like triumph is elevated enormously by knockout performances.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: Director David O. Russell ("Three Kings") delivers a film of staggering impact.

author: Rick Groen
quote: The picture makes too many concessions to the Hollywood judges, pulls too many punches. But at least it has real punches to pull, because there's honest sweat here too, and a full complement of those archetypes that lie at the popular heart of the genre.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: If you think "Rocky" and "Raging Bull" define the alpha and omega of boxing movies, think again. David O. Russell's The Fighter proves there's still punch in the genre, especially when a filmmaker tells a familiar story in a brand-new way.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Bale dominates the movie as Dicky Eklund, a pathetic loudmouth who's let his own fight career slip away from him, yet what really holds this together is Wahlberg's low-key, firmly internalized performance as a man torn between his loyalty to the clan and his responsibility to himself.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: An off-center but exceptional boxing film I prefer in every aspect, especially one: It feels like it comes from real life as well as the movies.

author: Steven Rea
quote: The Fighter is funny, ferocious, sad, sweet, pulpy, and violent. Sometimes, all in the same minute.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Ultimately, The Fighter loses its courage and betrays the terms of its own story by fashioning an interpretation designed to please the people it portrays. It does a switch on us, by changing its focus from Micky's character to Micky's career and then pretending it was really about the career all along.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It's a film possessed of its own force, wit and style, and it builds to a rousing climax that absolutely pays off in crowd-pleasing fashion.  It knows what it is, doesn't try to be what it's not, and hits you with drop-dead force.  In short, it's terrific.

author: Steve Persall
quote: A boxing movie swinging in too many directions at once, as if someone sneaked a third clubber into the ring. All the emotional punches land solidly, to occasionally devastating effect, but at the conclusion you're not sure which competing cliche wins.

author: Roger Moore
quote: That rare film in which every performer in it leaves the viewer in awe.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: David O. Russell's film makes use of some terrific performances - Christian Bale is brilliant, as is Melissa Leo, even by their lofty standards.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: The weakness of the film is the weakness of the leading role. That's not a criticism of Mark Wahlberg, who has a quite capable range, but of how he and Russell see the character.

author: David Edelstein
quote: The movie has so much texture that once it gets you, you're good and got.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: By far the most rousing, expertly cast movie this year, David O. Russell's movie takes a roundabout way of telling its true story.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: Pity the boxing movie that thinks it can be both "Raging Bull" and "Rocky."

author: Jeannette Catsoulis
quote: At its best, The Fighter takes on the chasm between televised boxing and its mostly working-class, aspirational origins with grit and intelligence.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Russell has always excelled at finding new ways to use familiar actors, and every performance in The Fighter is noteworthy if not outstanding.

author: Peter Travers
quote: The Fighter, its heart full to bursting, is an emotional powerhouse that comes close to spilling over.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Cast against type is Amy Adams. Normally tabbed for sweet and innocent roles, Adams here dons the persona of a confrontational bitch.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Only Amy Adams, playing Mickey's tough-tender girlfriend Char­lene, manages to be convincingly working-class without seeming either dopey or rabid or strung-out.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: A movie so rousing, so real and so full of complicated emotions that it all feels brand-new.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: Bale's live-wire performance typifies the many major and minor elements that elevate The Fighter from the deeply conventional sports movie it might have been into the endearingly offbeat sports movie it turns out to be.

author: Ty Burr
quote: The Fighter is this close to a triumph: a movie that steeps us in the grit of its time and place - Lowell, Mass., in the 1990s - and electrifyingly dramatizes Ward's battles with the family that almost loved him to death.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: With solid bodywork, clever feints and tremendous heart, it scores at least a TKO, by which I mean both that it falls just short of overpowering greatness - I can't quite exclaim, "It's a knockout!" - and that the most impressive thing about it is technique.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: The rousing The Fighter tries a number of risky maneuvers and manages to make them pay off in the end. The movie initially feels like more of a near thing than the filmmakers anticipated, but as in boxing it's only the final decision that counts.

author: Mary Pols
quote: The screenplay, credited to three writers, has that over-doctored feeling to it, and we're asked to take on a larger redemption tale that undermines the truth of Bale's wholly unsympathetic portrayal of a drug addict and a narcissist. The Fighter's desire to show us what that awful combination looks like is overwhelmed by its urge to show us a Hollywood-style triumph.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: While the film handles itself well in the ring, it's brilliant in the arena of a blue-collar family that brutalizes its younger son and best hope for worldly success in the name of sustaining him.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Visionary director David O. Russell so deftly weaves the family's story that we, too, are initially seduced by Dicky.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: This one, as thoughtful as it is rousing, scores a TKO.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The tragedy of The Fighter is that Wahlberg's performance suggests a character who wants more. And yet Russell barely seems to notice how much subtlety Wahlberg brings to his role, or to the movie at large.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: It plays as a "Rocky"-fied fairy tale for our time: Consigned to Palookaville, a sweet, unassuming boxer with more heart than brains steps up-all the way to the top of the world.

author: Rex Reed
quote: These are characters so repulsive that it's hard to care what happens to them, but it's to the credit of a superb cast that you do end up caring.

author: David Fear
quote: You can't necessarily blame Wahlberg, as his modest performance is the one element that feels truly authentic and heartfelt.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: The Fighter, for all the dedication of its players, takes a heavy swing at us, and misses.

author: Amy Nicholson
quote: In its small moments, say when Walhberg sighs that his robe misspells "Micky," The Fighter feels clued-in to the very small, very tough world of a man trying to make his way out of his block-and after getting to know his family, you want to help him pack his bags.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: So like much of this film, the viewer is turned into an observer. You never feel close enough to the action, either in the ring or in the kitchens, living rooms and tough streets where the story takes place. The characters engage you up to a point but never really pull you in.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: If The Fighter feels like kind of a mess, lurching from one scene to the next as if the film itself has taken a few hits to the head, that's not entirely a bad thing.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: In terms of sheer originality, ambition and achievement, Inception is the movie of the summer, the movie of the year and the movie of our dreams.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: In a summer of remakes, reboots and sequels comes Inception, easily the most original movie idea in ages.

author: Justin Chang
quote: If Inception is a metaphysical puzzle, it's also a metaphorical one: It's hard not to draw connections between Cobb's dream-weaving and Nolan's filmmaking -- an activity devoted to constructing a simulacrum of reality, intended to seduce us, mess with our heads and leave a lasting impression. Mission accomplished.

author: Nev Pierce
quote: With physics-defying, thunderous action, heart-wringing emotion and an astonishing performance from DiCaprio, Nolan delivers another true original: welcome to an undiscovered country.

author: David Edelstein
quote: Inception manages to be clunky and confusing on four separate levels of reality.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: As engrossing and logic-resistant as the state of dreaming it seeks to replicate, Christopher Nolan's audacious new creation demands further study to fully absorb the multiple, simultaneous stories Nolan finagles into one narrative experience.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: Inception, though, is no "Avatar"--instead, it’s the movie that many wanted "Avatar" to be. In a roaringly fast first hour, we’re introduced to a new technology that allows for the bodily invasion of another person’s dreamworld.

author: Peter Travers
quote: In this wildly ingen­ious chess game, grandmaster Nolan plants ideas in our heads that disturb and dazzle. The result is a knockout. But be warned: Inception dreams big. How cool is that?

author: Nick Pinkerton
quote: It's obvious that Nolan either can't articulate or doesn't believe in a distinction between living feelings and dreams--and his barren Inception doesn't capture much of either.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Inception is an elegant, portentous ride, though I’m not sure Nolan is any closer to visualizing the real (dream) deal than Hitchcock was.

author: Rex Reed
quote: I'd like to tell you just how bad Inception really is, but since it is barely even remotely lucid, no sane description is possible.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Nolan blurs the distinction between dreams and reality so artfully that Inception may well be a masterpiece masquerading as a summer blockbuster.

author: Steve Persall
quote: A sensory and intellectual overload from start to finish, a brawny, brainy summer movie that may infuriate as many viewers as it enraptures.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: The visuals are stunning, perhaps the most fully realized of any film.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Here, finally, is something you've really never seen before.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Everything he (Nolan) does is forced and overthought, and Inception, far from being his ticket into hall-of-fame greatness, is a very expensive-looking, elephantine film whose myriad so-called complexities -- of both the emotional and intellectual sort -- add up to a kind of ADD tedium.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: Nolan’s end-act pacing has always felt ponderous – but it’s not enough to ruin what is surely the most intellectually and viscerally engaging action film in years. The soul doesn’t stir, no, but everything else is wildly somersaulting.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Inception does a difficult thing. It is wholly original, cut from new cloth, and yet structured with action movie basics so it feels like it makes more sense than (quite possibly) it does.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: One of the best things about Nolan as a director is that he’s not self-conscious. His movies unfold and fold in on themselves without the strain of labor or flash. But that lack of self-consciousness is also Nolan’s downside.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Stymied by a clunking script, crammed with expository exchanges and urgent blather.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: For the most part "Inception" is a handsome, clever and grindingly self-serious boy-movie, shorn of imagination, libido, spirituality or emotional depth. Nolan establishes a fascinating world, loaded with trapdoors, symbols and hidden secrets, and then squanders the opportunity on an overpriced "Twilight Zone" episode.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: The film is an imposing, prismatic achievement, and strongly resistant to an insta-reaction; when it’s over, Nolan still seems a few steps ahead of us.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The film is easier to admire than to fully grasp or be moved by it. Still, it's worth surrendering to the dream.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Inception delivers dazzling special effects and a boatload of stars, but it sags and eventually buckles under the weight of its complicated premise.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Inception is precisely the kind of brainy, ambitious, grand-scale adventure Hollywood should be making more of.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: At the end of Inception, I hadn't lived through the grueling emotional journey Nolan seemed to think I had, but I'd seen a bunch of cool images and admired some technically ambitious feats of filmmaking.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The film is Nolan's labyrinth all the way, and it's gratifying to experience a summer movie with large visual ambitions and with nothing more or less on its mind than (as Shakespeare said) a dream that hath no bottom.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Inception is that rare film that can be enjoyed on superficial and progressively deeper levels, a feat that uncannily mimics the mind-bending journey its protagonist takes.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: When was the last time you had your mind blown by a movie? Because when Inception ends and the lights come up, you'll be sitting in your seat, staring at the screen, wondering what the hell just happened.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: A tremendously exciting science-fiction thriller that's as disturbing as it sounds. This is a popular entertainment with a knockout punch so intense and unnerving it'll have you worrying if it's safe to close your eyes at night.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: Though there is a lot to see in Inception, there is nothing that counts as genuine vision. Mr. Nolan’s idea of the mind is too literal, too logical, too rule-bound to allow the full measure of madness -- the risk of real confusion, of delirium, of ineffable ambiguity -- that this subject requires.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The versatile actor brings the full weight of his talent to bear on a difficult role. DiCaprio has to hint at unpleasant secrets in Cobb's past while forging a bond with the audience. It's up to the performer to make Inception more about human beings than about special effects. He succeeds and that's one reason why this movie isn't only about challenging ideas and eye candy.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Nolan’s tale is not only a trip through mental labyrinths but a reminder that memories may cripple us, unless we learn to let them go.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: This is spellbinding, transporting, damn near indescribable and the latest indication that Christopher Nolan might be the slyest narrative tactician making movies today.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: This is a deeply felt work of art in the form of a big, brassy movie-movie.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: For movie buffs, the only real fun to be had at Inception could be toting up the lifts from other movies, including Cocteau’s “Blood of a Poet” and “The Matrix” series and just about anything by Kubrick.

author: Mike Scott
quote: What Nolan has created with Inception is the rare movie that is bound to improve with repeated viewings, both as a means to drink in its brilliance one more time, and to see what sly clues might have flown under your radar the first time around.

author: Rick Groen
quote: You can’t feel for anyone when nothing feels real. Memo to Christopher Nolan for future outings: Kill the dream, tell a story.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Christopher Nolan's "Memento" was a movie-lover's dream come true, a puzzle that was engaging both intellectually and emotionally. But his Inception is a wake-up call, a blaring reminder that cheap tricks can't compensate for personal investment.

author: Staff (Not credited)
quote: Inception is one of the best sci-fi movies of the new century, a mind-bender about dreams as public spaces.

author: David Denby
quote: Inception, is an astonishment, an engineering feat, and, finally, a folly.

author: John Anderson
quote: By convoluting the various planes of experience, by overlapping and obscuring ostensible realities and ostensible dreams, Mr. Nolan deprives us the opportunity of investing emotionally in any of it.

author: Anna Smith
quote: A witty, warm exploration of family life that's conventional and unconventional in equal measure.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: This warm, funny, sexy, smart movie erases the boundaries between specialized ''gay content'' and universal ''family content'' with such sneaky authority.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: Witty, urbane and thoroughly entertaining.

author: David Edelstein
quote: The self-satire of The Kids Are All Right is so knowing, so rich, so hilarious, so damn healthy that it blows all thoughts of degeneracy out of your head.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Though The Kids Are All Right sometimes smacks of political correctness, Cholodenko succeeds brilliantly in making her little clan seem completely run-of-the-mill.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: "All right" doesn't begin to describe it. The Kids Are All Right is wonderful. Here is a film that respects and enjoys all of its characters, the give-and-take and recklessness and wisdom of any functioning family unit, conventional or un-.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: Every scene has its highlights, from amusing observations about sex to poignant truths about parenting and partnerships. But what you'll remember most is the exquisitely lovely final scene, in which Cholodenko reminds us that all we need is a single moment of perfection -in a family, or even in a film - to believe that somehow, things will always be all right.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Like her (Cholodenko) other movies, this one has vivid characters and strong performances and flows like a slice of life set in an appealing, interesting world. But this one also has a good story and, if you're paying attention, a distinct point of view.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: It is outrageously funny without ever exaggerating for comic effect, and heartbreaking with only minimal melodramatic embellishment.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: A thrillingly funny and casually profound film.

author: Ty Burr
quote: Movies like The Kids Are All Right -- beautifully written, impeccably played, funny and randy and true -- don't come along very often.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: More universal than it is alternative, except in one sense: There's nothing else on the contemporary movie landscape like it.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: The movie we've been waiting for all year: a comedy that doesn't take cheap shots, a drama that doesn't manipulate, a movie of ideas that doesn't preach. It's a rich, layered, juicy film, with quiet revelations punctuated by big laughs.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: The Kids Are All Right probably could have used a few more scenes to come to an even more satisfying conclusion. But it's a terrific film anyway.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: Cholodenko's casually observant style perfectly matches the cast's thoughtful work, though the film ultimately proves more successful at creating messy situations than trying to resolve them.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: The Kids are All Right, a grin-cracking great portrait of a modern American family in minor and then major crises.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: It's a classic Hollywood domestic comedy with a mischievous twist.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: The Kids Are All Right ranks with the most compelling portraits of an American marriage, regardless of sexuality, in film history.

author: Roger Moore
quote: That humor is a the delicious underpinning to whatever melodrama happens as these five connect and clash. And that humor is what reassures us, even at its darkest moments, that no matter how things work out for the adults, these kids are going to be all right.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Cholodenko, whose previous features include the pretentious "High Art" and the sudsy "Laurel Canyon," pitches The Kids Are All Right at right level - there's enough light comedy to leaven the melodrama and keep it from becoming overbearing.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Moore and Bening are superb actors here, evoking a marriage of more than 20 years, and all of its shadings and secrets, idealism and compromise.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Here are five gifted actors at the top of their games as five characters in search of what makes a family.

author: Peter Travers
quote: The actors are to die for. Bening and Moore nail every nuance of a relationship going adrift. And Ruffalo is dynamite as a man keeping himself at a distance. Kids makes its own special magic. It's irresistible

author: Claudia Puig
quote: This gem features five topnotch, multidimensional performances in one of this summer's most engaging films.

author: Kerry Lengel
quote: Mark Ruffalo, in just the right amount of stubble, grease and leather, plays Paul, about as cool an instant dad as a SoCal kid named Laser could hope for.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Positioned somewhere between sitcom and piercing human drama, The Kids Are All Right, is both overtly familiar and cutting edge.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Ruefully funny, beautifully acted comedy of manners.

author: Steve Persall
quote: What makes Lisa Cholodenko's The Kids Are All Right remarkable also makes it a tad humdrum, which may be the filmmaker's point.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Arrives as the perfect midsummer movie, a comedy about a flawed-but-functional family that, like "Toy Story 3," captures the drama of growth and separation in all its exhilaration and heartache.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: I spent The Kids are All Right wondering whether director Lisa Cholodenko was affectionate toward her self-absorbed characters or gently mocking them. In the end, I thought she was both and liked the film more.

author: Rex Reed
quote: Some people might blindly and inaccurately accuse this movie of attacking family values, but it has exactly the opposite effect. Touching and funny in their upheaval, the people in The Kids Are All Right open the door to a brand new examination of family values that leaves you charged and cheering.

author: Mike Scott
quote: A thoroughly and unmistakably modern film so rooted in the now that it's bound to be remembered as a cinematic landmark.

author: Justin Lowe
quote: This love letter to gay-marriage supporters is respectably entertaining filmmaking, it's just not exceptional.

author: Rob Nelson
quote: Sparked by wonderfully lived-in performances from Julianne Moore and Mark Ruffalo, The Kids Are All Right is alright, if not up to the level of writer-director Lisa Cholodenko's earlier pair of new bohemian dramas, "High Art" and "Laurel Canyon."

author: J. Hoberman
quote: Serious comedy, powered by an enthusiastic cast and full of good-natured innuendo, Lisa Cholodenko's The Kids Are All Right gives adolescent coming-of-age and the battle of the sexes a unique twist.

author: Ray Greene
quote: The soul of the movie is Mia Wasikowska, a radiant young actress who captures with quiet precision the quandary of a bookish "good girl" suddenly roused to wider personal and experiential possibilities, and to their potential cost.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Cholodenko casts much better than she writes. Yet, alas, even a talented veteran like Moore can't sell a hoary line like, "Sometimes you hurt the ones you love the most." Maybe if she'd set it to music – nope, sorry, that's already been done.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: There are not only glancing moments but whole sequences in this movie when the agony of social embarrassment makes you want to haul the characters to their feet and slap them in the chops.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: Never quite shakes its sitcom-ish setup. The director alternates incident-laden storytelling with penetrating character moments that her terrific cast acts to the fullest.

author: Joe Williams
quote: The King's Speech is the epitome of prestige cinema, an impeccably crafted and emotionally compelling drama that deserves the many laurels it surely will receive.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: It's a "keep calm, carry on" wartime melodrama of the first order, and stiff though it may be, it is never less than brilliantly done.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Polished, thoughtful and touching.

author: Connie Ogle
quote: Tom Hooper's terrific, Oscar-worthy film is not merely a spot-on period piece; it's also a heartfelt study in the shadings of courage, a film about duty and friendship that's often warmly funny and sometimes painful to watch.

author: Mike Scott
quote: One of the chief reasons that director Tom Hooper's richly produced film works so well is because it operates on so many different levels. The King's Speech is all about layers, and Hooper keeps it humming on several at once.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It's a fine, absorbing work, built with brilliance and without excessive showiness or flash.  It feels, in fact, like a classic virtually upon its arrival.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Engrossing and moving story of a alternately warm and combative relationship.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Like Bertie's struggle, there's so much wonderment to articulate about this film that being mistaken for a stammering idiot is a risk. See it, then say it for yourself: The King's Speech is the best movie of 2010.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Delivers solid drama with a rousing climax - a fully satisfying and uplifting period piece that achieves its dramatic potential without sacrificing historical accuracy.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The actors, predictably, are superb in roles shaped by screenwriter David Seidler, and directed by Tom Hooper. Yet they are unpredictably superb as well.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: A host of British acting royalty, meanwhile, roams around the film: Derek Jacobi as the Archbishop of Canterbury, Claire Bloom as Queen Mary, Timothy Spall as Winston Churchill and so on.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: The two leads keep the movie afloat with their light-footed class warfare. This Anglican buddy romance is buoyed by a spicy history lesson about the scandalous marriage of the duke's elder brother, Edward VIII, to the twice-divorced Wallis Simpson.

author: Ty Burr
quote: The movie isn't badly done, just overdone - a cozy art-house crowd-pleaser coasting on the expectations of its genre.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Under Hooper's deft direction, it packs the suspense of a thriller.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: It's the kind of absorbing, attractive, unfailingly tasteful enterprise that a critic can recommend without caveat.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: What we have here is a superior historical drama and a powerful personal one.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: The gift of The King's Speech is that it allows us to look on as a pair of masterful actors re-create a monumental test of wills.

author: David Edelstein
quote: It's a prizewinning combination, terribly English and totally Hollywood, and Firth is, once more, uncanny: He evokes, in mid-stammer, existential dread.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: It's the relationship between the two men that makes the film work: Geoffrey Rush's teacher cracking the quip, and Colin Firth so persuasive as the panicky king that by the time he gets to his crucial speech about going to war, you'll be panicking right along with him.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: It's a warm, richly funny and highly enjoyable human story that takes an intriguing sideways glance at a crucial period in 20th-century history.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: The King's Speech is a warm, wise film - the best period movie of the year and one of the year's best movies.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Yes, The King's Speech is a lively burst of populist rhetoric, superbly performed and guaranteed to please even discriminating crowds.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: It perhaps started with "The Queen," continued with "Young Victoria" and now achieves the most intimate glimpse inside the royal camp to date with The King's Speech.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: That film does have its attractions, notably in its two solid leads and standout support from Mr. Pearce.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: The barbs of wit, delivered throughout, are like the retractable daggers used in stage productions of "Macbeth" or "Julius Caesar": they gleam enticingly, they plunge home to the hilt, but they leave no trace of a wound.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Obvious, though, is the word for Hopper's direction. It amplifies to rock-concert level every pained plosive in Bertie's speech, forces certain characters dangerously close to caricature.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: While far from easy, both roles provide a delightful opportunity for Firth and Rush to poke a bit of fun at their profession.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: A well-wrought, enjoyably amusing inspirational drama that successfully humanizes, even as it pokes fun at, the House of Windsor.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: No screen portrait of a king has ever been more stirring-heartbreaking at first, then stirring. That's partly due to the screenplay, which contains two of the best-written roles in recent memory, and to Mr. Hooper's superb direction.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: The King's Speech is a magnificent movie treat, one of the very best pictures of the year.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: A remarkable movie about a remarkable friendship. It honors the audience's intelligence, which makes it a double rarity.

author: Ian Nathan
quote: Think the blazing joys of "Chariots Of Fire" where the race is to the end of a sentence. Can it be that the British are coming?

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Lest the audience miss a cue, Hooper and soundtrack composer Alexandre Desplat count on the ringing grandeur of Beethoven's Seventh Symphony - the famous second movement, no less - to amp the emotions.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: A direct and heartfelt piece of work. It's conventional, maybe, in its sense of filmmaking decorum, but extraordinary in the way it cuts to the core of human frustration and feelings of inadequacy, reminding us how universal those feelings are.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Despite being about a royal family at a critical moment in history, The King's Speech doesn't shout about its many strengths. Rather, it urges you to lean in close, where its intelligence and heart come through loud and clear.

author: Rex Reed
quote: As the actor of the year in the film of the year, I can't think of enough adjectives to praise Firth properly. The King's Speech has left me speechless.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Old-school filmmaking at its best.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Two men alone create an epic landscape of feeling in one of the very best movies of the year.

author: Tasha Robinson
quote: The King's Speech is admirably free of easy answers and simple, happy endings; it's a skewed, awards-ready version of history, but one polished to a fine, satisfying shine.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: Firth is exceptional in letting us into his dissolving pride.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Let's say it without equivocation: Colin Firth deserves an Oscar for his lead role in The King's Speech as the stammering King George VI.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: With a thieves den of borderline-Shakespearian characters, a wickedly literate screenplay, potent direction by David Fincher, an exceptional ensemble cast and subject matter that speaks to a generation and well beyond, The Social Network is mesmerizing.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Keep your eyes on Garfield - he's shatteringly good, the soul of a film that might otherwise be without one. The Social Network is the movie of the year. But Fincher and Sorkin triumph by taking it further. Lacing their scathing wit with an aching sadness, they define the dark irony of the past decade.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: The film comes down to a mesmerizing portrait of a man who in any other age would perhaps be deemed nuts or useless, but in the Internet age has this mental agility to transform an idea into an empire.

author: Justin Chang
quote: Continues Fincher's fascinating transition from genre filmmaker extraordinaire to indelible chronicler of our times.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Quite possibly the first truly great fact-based movie of the 21st century.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: When Mr. Eisenberg makes Mark's face go blank, the character seems scarily emptied out: it's a subtly great, at times unsettling, performance.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: It's a grandly entertaining reminder of everything we used to go to the movies for (and still can't get online): sparkling dialogue, thorny situations, soulful performances, and an unusually open-ended and relevant engagement with a major social issue of the day: how we (dis)connect.

author: David Denby
quote: Brilliantly entertaining and emotionally wrenching.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Fincher and his screenwriter, TV writer-god Aaron Sorkin, have made a seemingly modest picture that achieves something close to greatness the old-fashioned, slow-burning way: By telling a story with faces, dialogue and body language of all types, from awkward to swaggering.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: The rewards for paying attention are mammoth and exhilarating. This is a high-IQ movie that gives viewers an IQ high.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: All I can say about Timberlake's performance as the thoroughly odious, desperately seductive, textbook-case metrosexual Parker is that he brings so much reptilian fun that he unbalances the movie, almost fatally.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Is director David Fincher's film the stuff of greatness? Not quite. But the picture is very, very good.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: Corny as that is, the film's nadir comes when Zuckerberg's pretty young lawyer comforts him (or us) with the mealy-mouthed observation, "You're not an asshole, Mark. You're just trying so hard to be one."

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: The power of The Social Network is that Zuckerberg is a weasel with a mission that can never be dismissed. The movie suggests that he may have built his ambivalence about human connection into Facebook's very DNA. That's what makes him a jerk-hero for our time.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: It's one of the best movies of the year, one of the best entries ever in the Way We Live Now oeuvre.

author: Ty Burr
quote: On the level of craft, the movie's just absurdly enjoyable. Sorkin's dialogue dazzles; the photography is burnished and sleek; the editing confidently sorts out a complex narrative.

author: Rex Reed
quote: This film transcends its trendy, obvious limitations with enough vitality and vitriol to make it as informative and breathless as it is entertaining.

author: Roger Moore
quote: The performances, direction and writing of one of the best pictures of 2010 make this Social Network every bit as addictive, and a little chilling as well.

author: Steve Persall
quote: This is a remarkable film for more reasons than its antihero, from the cyberspeed wisdom of Aaron Sorkin's screenplay to Jeff Cronenweth's camera prowling the excesses of youthful genius gone wild.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: David Fincher's film has the rare quality of being not only as smart as its brilliant hero, but in the same way. It is cocksure, impatient, cold, exciting and instinctively perceptive.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Smartly written by Aaron Sorkin, directed to within an inch of its life by David Fincher and anchored by a perfectly pitched performance by Jesse Eisenberg, The Social Network is a barn-burner of a tale that unfolds at a splendid clip.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Delivers the heady, rib-tickling rush of an action picture, and it gradually builds to an emotional wallop that blindsides you.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The film owes much of its success to the inspired pairing of Fincher and Sorkin.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: Blisteringly entertaining.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: The founding of Facebook becomes a tale for our times in this masterful social drama.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: David Fincher obsesses about obsessive people.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Most of all, it is the improbably entertaining story of how new media are altering the very nature of courtship and friendship.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: A work deeper than its nickname, "The Facebook Movie," hints at - coils around your brain. Weeks after seeing it, moments from it will haunt you.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: A film that's at once timely and timeless.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: Zuckerberg's story ends up feeling bigger than his own life.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: Terrific entertainment - an unlikely thriller that makes business ethics, class distinctions and intellectual-property arguments sexy, that zips through two hours quicker than you can say "relationship status," and that'll likely fascinate pretty much anyone not named Zuckerberg.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: The Social Network has understandably been compared to "Citizen Kane" in its depiction of a man who changes society through bending an emergent technology to his will.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: This account of Facebook's founder, and of the website's explosive growth, quickly lifts you to a state of exhilaration, and pretty much keeps you there for two hours.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Shot to shot, scene to scene, The Social Network nearly never puts a foot wrong or, really, does anything to make you feel less than compelled.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: But make no mistake, whether the movie is fair or horribly unfair - I know nothing of the actual facts and can't make that determination - its portrait of Zuckerberg is a hatchet job of epic and perhaps lasting proportions.

author: Rick Groen
quote: It has the staccato wit of a drawing-room comedy, the fatal flaw of a tragic romance and the buzzy immediacy of a front-page headline, all powered by a kinetic engine typically found in an action flick. And that's just the opening scene.

author: Mike Scott
quote: The result is a ripped-from-the-Zeitgeist film that is razor-sharp, an astute and funny portrait of the early 2000s, with all its LOL's, its IMO's and its WTF's. Mostly its WTF's.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Eisenberg, one of those young actors who has existed just below the radar for several years now (he was the lead in both "Zombieland" and "Adventureland," not to be confused with one another), deserves an Oscar for this dead-on portrayal of a temperamental genius.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: The Social Network wants to be a social satire, a miniaturist comedy of manners, and a Greek tragedy; it bites off a lot, at times more than it can chew. But even the unmasticated morsels are pretty tasty.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: The problem is, the geek in question, at least as Jesse Eisenberg plays him, doesn't have the emotional expansiveness to fill out a movie. Perhaps sensing this, the filmmakers play out the story line from multiple points of view and crowd the stage with a pageant of voluble supporting characters.

author: David Edelstein
quote: It's an entertainingly cynical small movie. Aaron Sorkin's dialogue tumbles out so fast it's as if the characters want their brains to keep pace with their processors; they talk like they keyboard, like Fincher directs, with no time for niceties.

author: Damon Wise
quote: A rich, understated character drama that gleefully exposes the petty playground politics at the centre of one of the internet-era's most bitter court cases.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: What MORE could audiences want from a movie than this hilarious, heartwarming entertainment for all ages?

author: Michael Rechtshaffen
quote: Woody, Buzz and playmates make a thoroughly engaging, emotionally satisfying return.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: This tertiary adventure delivers welcome yet nonessential fun, landing well after its creators have grown up and succeeded toying with more sophisticated stories.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: Toy Story 3 is very much a worthy entry in the series, a movie well worth making (and seeing). It continues the legacy. It just doesn't expand upon it.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Dazzling, scary and sentimental.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: Clearly, Pixar’s genius for adventurous storytelling continues unabated.

author: Eric Hynes
quote: TS3, like its predecessors, is a clever, engrossing adventure.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Toy Story 3 is a salute to the magic of making believe.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Toy Story 3 enhances the legacy of its brand while providing exceptional entertainment value for viewers of all ages, especially for those who favor the brighter, livelier 2-D iteration over the 3-D gimmick.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: I laughed more (sincerely, full-throatedly) at Toy Story 3’s smart comedy than at any other film of the still-young summer movie slate.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: This is a jolly, slapstick comedy, lacking the almost eerie humanity that infused the earlier “Toy Story” sagas, and happier with action and jokes than with characters and emotions.

author: Joe Williams
quote: It's a well-earned curtain call for some of the most beloved characters in one of the best-sustained feats of recent cinema.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Though uneven and less witty than the first two, Toy Story 3 delivers quite enough in two dimensions.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: It’s the first Pixar effort that feels less like a creative outpouring and more like an obligation met to satisfy a distribution schedule.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: A worthy and delirious final chapter to this hallowed animation franchise.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: While the vocal performances of Hanks, Allen and company make up a perfect ensemble, and its visual leaps astound, TS3's real power sneaks up on you.

author: Tasha Robinson
quote: The film never lets banter, visual gags, or the usual manic kid-flick running about interfere with its more delicately handled thoughts on loyalty, longing, broken relationships, and generational continuity. It honestly earns its emotion, moment by painstakingly executed moment.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: A sequel made with care and integrity, Toy Story 3 is just moving enough: It winds its way gently toward its big themes instead of grabbing desperately at them, and because its plot is so beautifully worked out, getting there is almost all of the fun.

author: Ty Burr
quote: It’s when Toy Story 3 becomes a jailbreak movie that it comes into its own.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Although Toy Story 3 plays with themes of aging and obsolescence, it's really a straight-ahead action pic, with the toys planning, and attempting, their escape and rescue missions. (Hey, it's The A-Team!)

author: Richard Corliss
quote: An instant classic.

author: Kristin Hunt
quote: The credits may be silly, but the last scene is a definite tear-jerker.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Lasseter and his team plunge the audience into a collective case of empty- nest syndrome, with a dash of mortal terror thrown in for grins. And again, they make it work.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: The third film of the trilogy turns out to be gorgeously joyous and deeply felt.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: This film becomes the kind of love note to movies we want and need.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: As sweet, as touching, as humane a movie as you are likely to see this summer.

author: Peter Travers
quote: A joy to behold.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: This installment, the best of the three, is everything a movie should be: hilarious, touching, exciting and clever.

author: M. E. Russell
quote: In the year's least surprising news, Toy Story 3 continues Pixar's near-perfect streak.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: A near-perfect piece of popular entertainment, a children's classic.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Eventually, Toy Story 3 finds its way back to that theme of the power of childhood play. There are a few worrisome moments en route, though, when not only the characters but the filmmakers seem to have lost their way.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Toy Story 3 is a better film than "Wall-E" and "Up" in that it succeeds completely in conventional terms. For 103 minutes, it never takes audience interest for granted. It has action, horror and vivid characters, and it always keeps moving forward.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: A bona fide summer delight loaded with action, humor, nostalgia, a veritable blizzard of pop-culture references and general good vibes.

author: Mike Scott
quote: Positively soars.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: May not be a masterpiece, but it still had me in tears at the end.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Toy Story 3, has more emotional power than either of its predecessors. Come to think of it, it also has more emotional power than most of the live-action movies out there.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Toy Story 3 isn't merely the best movie of the summer -- even with summer just kicking in -- but an immediate candidate for best of the year.

author: David Edelstein
quote: For grown-ups, the film will touch something deeper: the heartfelt wish that childhood memories will never fade.

author: Dan Jolin
quote: A kids’ movie for grown-ups. A grown-up movie for kids. Exactly what you’d expect -- and hope for -- from the latest, and we’re guessing final, Woody and Buzz adventure.

author: Angie Errigo
quote: Terrific: tough, exciting, funny, gorgeous and bewitchingly acted, this is darn close to perfection.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: The tense verbal comedy of Mattie's early negotiation with a Fort Smith merchant should win you over to this movie's high linguistic wit. If not, you may as well slip out of the theater and into "Little Fockers."

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: All this is not to say that the Coens' True Grit is an awful film; it's just that these filmmakers have set their own standards for excellence, and True Grit falls short.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: As usual, the Coens' visual elements are pristine. The contrasting colours in the fire-lit interiors are gorgeous, while cinematographer Roger Deakins keeps the camera close, resisting traditional panoramic views.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: A winning western with just a few dark eddies beneath the surface, one that features a star-making lead performance and some spectacular photography, but falls just short of being great.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: The first hour and half or so of True Grit is as good as anything the Coens have ever done-a sweeping Western that, like John Ford's best films, exposes the cracks in American myths of frontier justice and self-reliance.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Remaking a cherished movie is not, to borrow a fancy phrase from the dialogue, malum in se - wrong in itself - but there are always losses along with the changes and gains.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: For the most part, the Coens' is a highly enjoyable yarn, stocked with pungent bushwa and a full panoply of frontier bozos.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Mattie is a no-nonsense mite with a forthright manner and a mean head for figures; she wears her hair in two sturdy braids whose tips have never seen the inside of any inkwell, believe you me.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The original "True Grit" might have been eclipsed by John Wayne's larger-than-life persona, but the Coen brothers' remake is an ensemble piece that feels freshly their own.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: The Coens direct True Grit with a light touch, but like Portis' stark, funny novel, their adventure tale shaves off none of the rough edges.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: This remake by Joel and Ethan Coen is being positioned as a truer True Grit, and though they take their own liberties with the plot and tone, they preserve Portis's impeccably authentic dialogue, which does more to conjure up the Arkansas of the 1870s than any period trappings.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: A wonderfully entertaining, beautiful Western drama that lets the quirks of the genre gallop freely as it keeps a tight rein throughout.

author: Mike Scott
quote: This is a dirty, stinky Western -- the kind where authenticity is the guiding artistic hand and where a layer of filth and grime have seemingly settled over everything but the popcorn in your lap.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: A fine, straightforward and engaging film that restores the salt, fire and humor that Hathaway and company drained from their source, Charles Portis' wonderful 1968 novel.

author: Steve Persall
quote: True Grit is a very good movie that might be more embraceable if we didn't know who was pulling the trigger.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: If there's one big difference between this version and the old, it's in the attitude toward violence. The new version may be more graphic, but it doesn't present violence as inevitable or necessary, just ugly.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: True Grit has sweep and scope and entertainment value to burn, but it's Mattie who invests even the grandest aesthetic elements with meaning.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Matt Damon is an interesting, chatty choice to play  Laboeuf.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: This is a film by the Coen Brothers, and this is the first straight genre exercise in their career. It's a loving one. Their craftsmanship is a wonder. Their casting is always inspired and exact. The cinematography by Roger Deakins reminds us of the glory that was, and can still be, the Western.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The biggest change from the '69 "True Grit" is the best thing about this formidably well-crafted picture. Portis's narrator and heroine, 14-year-old Mattie Ross, runs the show this time, not the one-eyed marshal.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: A gorgeously shot, well-acted Western that resonates more the more you let it settle.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Those of us who admire Charles Portis' novel have waited 40 years for a screen version that's as literal as possible – and the Coen brothers just about deliver it.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: This isn't a rousing movie as much as a reassurance. The brothers (Coens) prove they can play it straight, but they're preferred, for better and worse, at a sharp angle.

author: Steven Rea
quote: True Grit is probably the least ironic picture in the Coen Brothers' worthy canon, but that doesn't mean it's devoid of their signature oddities, that it doesn't take a few dark, strange turns.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: The best way to approach Joel and Ethan Coen's eagerly awaited True Grit is to lower your expectations, then lower them a bit more. The problem is not the movie, which is a terrific, no-nonsense, straightforward western. The surprise – or vague disappointment – is the prevailing lack of Coen-ness in the movie.

author: Joe Williams
quote: True Grit is just a couple bloody gunfights removed from an old-fashioned Disney yarn. Yet it's still unmistakably a Coen brothers movie, from the stray weirdness of a bearskin-clad dentist to the bulls-eye delights of the dialogue.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: On its own conventional terms, the film succeeds – maybe not as a "Coen Brothers" movie, but as a tall tale well told.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: When Iris DeMent's impeccable version of the hymn is heard on the soundtrack as the final credits roll, it's the perfect touch to end a film whose aim is always true.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: It isn't much of a contest: The clear winner is John Wayne, because the Coens are playing his game. The Duke couldn't do the Coens' sly in-jokes, but they've never been able to reach out and move the audience to heights of emotion. Before now, they've never tried.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: In some ways, much like Charles Laughton's "Night of the Hunter," which the Coens quote both musically and visually, True Grit is a parable about good and evil. Only here, the lines between the two are so blurred as to be indistinguishable, making this a true picture of how the West was won, or - depending on your view - lost.

author: Peter Travers
quote: What makes True Grit a new classic for the Coens is the way the brothers absorb the unfairly unsung Portis into their DNA, like they did with Cormac McCarthy in "No Country for Old Men." True Grit is packed with action and laughs, plus a touching coda with an older Mattie, but it's the dialogue that really sings. Great filmmaking. Great acting. Great movie. Saddle up.

author: Rex Reed
quote: The original western won John Wayne a puzzling and undeserved Oscar for finally falling off his horse. Don't expect the same miracle for Jeff Bridges. In the numbing hands of pretentious filmmakers Joel and Ethan Coen, history does not repeat itself in any way whatsoever.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The Coens have fashioned one of the best Westerns in recent years - a modern reworking of a classic that never feels superfluous.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Truer than the John Wayne showpiece and less gritty than the book, this True Grit is just tasty enough to leave movie lovers hungry for a missing spice.

author: David Fear
quote: To make a Western now is in itself a subversive act. Improving, embellishing and reclaiming an old-fashioned oater from the vintage studio-cheese bin with such humor and vigor seems truly, truly ballsy.

author: David Edelstein
quote: True Grit isn't as momentous an event as you might hope, but once you adjust to its deliberate rhythms (it starts slowly), it's a charming, deadpan Western comedy.

author: David Denby
quote: For the Coens, the plot elements are a given; the telling is all. [20 & 27 Dec. 2010, p. 144]

author: Pete Hammond
quote: Smartly emphasizing Portis' quirky dialogue and dark comic tone, the Coens show the flare that made them famous.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Well-made and acted Coen Brothers remake lacks the humor and resonance that might have made it memorable.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: Rather than a case of the Dude doing the Duke, Bridges' irascible old cuss is a genuine original who feels larger than the familiar saga that contains him.

author: Ray Greene
quote: Winter's Bone so far past any notion of formula or precedent that comparison is a futile exercise. This film is a thing all its own.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Winter's Bone is unforgettable. It means to shake you, and does.

author: John DeFore
quote: Grim backwoods tale takes its time building momentum.

author: Justin Chang
quote: Raw but utterly enveloping.

author: David Edelstein
quote: For all the horror, it's the drive toward life, not the decay, that lingers in the mind. As a modern heroine, Ree Dolly has no peer, and Winter's Bone is the year's most stirring film.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Debra Granik's Winter's Bone is one of those movies -- like last year's inner-city down-a-thon, "Precious" -- that can't quite make a distinction between profundity and plain old bleakness.

author: Dan Kois
quote: In the end, the most interesting aspect of this quiet, sometimes frustrating, sometimes thrilling film is the way it teases out the intricate power structures that flourish even in as godforsaken (and lovely) a place as the Ozarks.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: Again, Granik has foregrounded a bold woman, expertly balanced between fearlessness and Ree's own private nervousness.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: One of the unshowiest and most true-blooded epics of Americana you're ever likely to see.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: Granik has no taste for noir archness, opting for a chilly, shot-on-decaying-locations naturalism that feels as lived-in as Lawrence's performance.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: On its face, Winter's Bone, like "Down to the Bone," is a bleakly realist drama about a community decimated by poverty and hopelessness, yet bound together by deep ties of class, gender and blood.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Spectacular for its humanity, austere beauty and heart-stopping urgency.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Every so often a film gets under our skin with its haunting authenticity, reinforcing our faith in the wonderfully transporting power of cinematic storytelling. Winter's Bone is unquestionably that film.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Intense, immersive and in control, Winter's Bone has an art house soul inside a B picture body, and that proves to be a potent combination indeed.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: The film's realism is a point of entry rather than the whole point of the exercise. Its setting is finally subordinate to the main character, as memorable and vivid a heroine as you are likely to see on screen this season.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Amid all the hokey hill stuff, Lawrence's hard eyes and manner draw us in.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: The main reason for Winter's Bone to exist is that it delivers a little voyeuristic thrill -- a bit of poverty porno -- for the critics who awarded it their highest honors at this year's Sundance Film Festival.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: This movie's an absolute knockout. I know it's only June, but I'm damned if this isn't the breakthrough American film of the year.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: The movie heroes who affect me most are not extroverted. They don't strut, speechify and lead armies. They have no superpowers. They are ordinary people who are faced with a need and rise to the occasion. Ree Dolly is such a hero.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Winter's Bone is the best film of the year.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Winter's Bone often seems to be unfolding in a world apart, with its own moral logic and codes of conduct. It might feel like prison if it weren't so obviously home.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The way Lawrence captures a young woman's fear and resolve, often non-verbally, well … this is a considerable talent well on her way to a great career. It's for performances like this that moviegoers find themselves taking a chance on a title that doesn't have a fast-food tie-in.

author: Ty Burr
quote: It's those noir bones that give this social-realist drama its punch, as if Humphrey Bogart had been recast as a 17-year-old girl and dropped into the poorest corner of America.

author: Steven Rea
quote: It's been a long time since a film has conveyed a culture, and a sense of place, with such telling precision. At the same time, Winter's Bone thrums with suspense.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: A tough movie to love.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: This is one helluva drama, with one helluva star turn by Jennifer Lawrence as Ree.

author: Rick Groen
quote: This is a world out of time and, despite the trappings of flinty realism, the film too unfolds like an elemental myth from the stormy past – a Greek tragedy driven by dark fates and struggling toward a catharsis.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Granik filmed in actual locations and enlisted many locals as actors. They blend unobtrusively with the professionals in the cast.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Winter's Bone is a welcome reminder that thrillers don't have to be loud and boisterous to grab the attention and keep it captive.

author: Marc Mohan
quote: With a level-gazed approach to its milieu, empathetic but clear-eyed, Winter's Bone practically makes up for 40 years of "Deliverance"-style hillbilly cartoons.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Winter's Bone never hits a false note.

author: David Denby
quote: In its lived-in, completely non-ideological way, Winter's Bone is one of the great feminist works in film.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Lawrence is in every scene of Winter's Bone, leaving her plenty of opportunity to make false moves. I dare you to find one, in a performance to be remembered during awards season.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: A genuine triumph, a great movie with astounding performances so natural, so genuine, that you forget it's a movie.

author: Mike Scott
quote: Like "The Hurt Locker," Winter's Bone is a spare but riveting drama with a female director. It is built around a raw, revelatory performance by a young, little-known lead actor.

author: Roger Moore
quote: This unblinking look at America's Red State Crystal Meth Belt is an instant Southern Gothic classic.

author: Connie Ogle
quote: It's not always an easy movie to watch, but its characters are unforgettable.

author: Philip Wilding
quote: A vivid reworking of Daniel Woodrell's novel that brings the book's conflicted heroine to searing life in a piece of unhurried filmmaking too rarely seen these days.

author: Mike Scott
quote: If nothing else, this is a cinematic high-wire act.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Hazanavicius isn't just making a "silent movie," he is attempting to enter a time warp and craft something that would fool all but the most studious and scholarly into believing it could have been a lost film from a bygone era. If his tongue is sometimes a little in his cheek, that's all part of the fun.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: The Artist is such an engaging, delightful film that, if you like movies, you will walk out of the theater with a smile. You just will; it's that inspired.

author: Marc Savlov
quote: What's so intensely pleasurable about The Artist, however, is not its predetermined seriocomic trajectory but the endless parade of smartly creative and self-referential gags, which include all manner of sly, silent delights; the inevitable Jack Russell; and even an extended orchestral cue of Bernard Herrmann's, cribbed outright from "Vertigo."

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Talkies may have killed silent movies, the way TV serials and soap operas wiped out radio dramas. But there are stories most effectively told in the old style, and The Artist is proof.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: This effort often manages to duplicate the magical pantomime of the era; a lovely scene in which Bejo drapes herself in the arms of a hung jacket as if it were a human lover could have come straight out of a Marion Davies picture.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: The Artist may not be great art, but it's pearly entertainment.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Hazanavicius crafted more than a replica of the silent era; this feels like a time capsule found 80 years later, right on time to be revolutionary in a louder world. Yet The Artist is a masterwork that likely won't be imitated. How many movies in 2011 can you say that about? Only the best one.

author: Ty Burr
quote: Michael Hazanavicius's love letter to classic cinema isn't perfect but it's close enough to make just about anyone who sees it ridiculously happy - and that includes children and grown-ups who have never come across a silent film.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Strangely, wonderfully, The Artist feels as bold and innovative a moviegoing experience as James Cameron's bells-and-whistles Avatar did a couple of years ago. Retro becomes nuevo. Quaint becomes cool.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: In this film, a single word is worth more than all the expensive effects imaginable.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It's a bit precious, yes, but its earnestness and joy carry you along, and its climax simply delights.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Artist evinces unlimited love for the look and ethos of the 1920s as well for the style of the movies. The filmmakers clearly did their homework and took great pleasure in doing so, an enjoyment that is passed along in ample doses to any viewer game for their nifty little conceit.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Drama, comedy, action and romance are intertwined in this gorgeously photographed and brilliantly directed film. Lead performances are thoroughly engaging despite - or perhaps because of - being wordless.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: It combines delightful humor and charm with what movies at their best have always conveyed: the honest power of pure emotion. It is a movie love story and a love note to the movies, all at the same time.

author: Joe Williams
quote: May be too cute to qualify as high art, but it's highly entertaining.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Here is one of the most entertaining films in many a moon, a film that charms because of its story, its performances and because of the sly way it plays with being silent and black and white.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: In the long and fraught history of Franco-American cultural relations, this movie is more than a peace offering; it's a loving, goofy, joyous French kiss.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: This slight but enormously likable picture seems destined to be an awards magnet.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: In many ways - in all ways - The Artist is a profound achievement.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Silence makes the film interesting by enticing us to concentrate in ways we're not used to, while artistry carries the day. The Artist may have started as a daring stunt, but it elevates itself to an endearing - and probably enduring - delight.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: It's a picture that romances its audience into watching in a new way - by, paradoxically, asking us to watch in an old way. The Artist is perhaps the most modern movie imaginable right now.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Literally the kind of movie they just don't make anymore, Michel Hazanavicius' French-sponsored charmer The Artist is a gorgeous black-and-white love letter to silent Hollywood with old-fashioned English intertitles and just a single line of audible (English) dialogue.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: This is not a work of film history but rather a generous, touching and slightly daffy expression of unbridled movie love.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: The delighted gasps in the theater will make you glad you took a chance on The Artist. Silent black-and-white movies are not coming back, but this one is such a rewarding labor of love by all of the artists involved that it just might make you wish they could.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: The Artist is full of homages to many other films. I suppose it will be fun for cinéastes to pick out the references, but not all of them – like the ones from "Citizen Kane" or "Sunset Boulevard" – are especially germane.

author: Eric Kohn
quote: The Artist plays around with the distinction between silent and sound cinema, resulting in the superficial entertainment value of a high concept film school joke. But it's a charming and supremely gorgeous joke -- sometimes too clever for its own good, other times not clever enough, and always at least an attractive diversion.

author: Mary Pols
quote: Beyond its craftiness and impeccable craft, the film sparks a warm connection with the viewer. Like a smiling cavalier swinging into view to rescue an imperiled maiden, The Artist brings salvation to melancholy movie lovers. For here is that rare film indeed that offers pleasure beyond words.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Days after I saw The Artist, I was still thinking (and grinning) about it, because the movie's real romance is the one between us, the jaded 21st-century audience, and the mechanical innocence of old movies, which here becomes new again.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: The Artist is anything but mute, with a lush orchestral score and a little sonic wink at the the end; fewer movies this year reward listening - and watching - so lavishly.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: With elements of "A Star Is Born" and "Singing in the Rain," The Artist is a rarity, an ingenious crowd-pleaser.

author: Rex Reed
quote: Get ready for a smash hit. Gimmicky but delicious, this is a valentine to the movies I promise you will cherish.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: A fascinating experiment is about to happen, and who doesn't want to be part of a little fun? That rarest of birds - a b&w silent film - is set to swoop into multiplexes. Trust us, it won't bite.

author: Tasha Robinson
quote: It's a beautifully shot, beautifully acted piece of fluff.

author: Melissa Anderson
quote: The Artist is movie love at its most anodyne; where Guy Maddin has used the conventions of silent film to express his loony psychosexual fantasias for more than a decade, Hazanavicius sweetly asks that we not be afraid of the past.

author: Peter Travers
quote: The Artist encapsulates everything we go to movies for: action, laughs, tears and a chance to get lost in another world. It just might leave you speechless. How can Oscar resist?

author: Peter Debruge
quote: A love letter to silent cinema sealed with a smirk, The Artist reteams director Michel Hazanavicius with dapper "OSS 117" star Jean Dujardin for another high-concept homage, delivering a heartfelt, old-school romance without the aid of spoken dialogue or sound.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: The Artist is not just about black-and-white silent pictures. It is a black-and-white silent picture. And it's French.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: The film's charm and delight of discovery, plus its sterling international performances, could make it a breakout hit in theaters.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: A brazen stunt that pays off. Writer-director Michel Hazanavicius, simultaneously channeling "Singin' in the Rain" and "A Star is Born," tells a story about 1920s Hollywood made in the style of that era.

author: Jaime N. Christley
quote: The Artist neatly sidesteps this unsolvable dilemma by ignoring everything that's fascinating and memorable about the era, focusing instead on a patchwork of general knowledge, so eroded of inconvenient facts that it doesn't even qualify as a roman à clef.

author: Dan Jolin
quote: A marvellous follow-up to 2004's "Sideways" - well worth the wait.

author: Steve Persall
quote: The Descendants would still be a splendid movie without him; with Clooney, it's one of 2011's very best.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: The Descendants is beautifully shot (by Phedon Papamichael) and compellingly performed, especially by its young stars, and it has moments of startling tenderness. If only it didn't feel phony to its bones.

author: Mike Scott
quote: At times humorous, at times poignant, but always absorbing.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: There are ample opportunities for the film to soak in pathos, righteousness, farce, or pictorialism, and Payne manages to nod at those pitfalls without falling into them.  In a way, it's just like Matt King's world: enviably plush but filled with the real pain of real life.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: All of Payne's films have been driven by the anger and frustration of his protagonists, but The Descendants is the first one in which sadness lurks behind every frame.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: It is particularly rewarding to see Clooney outside his comfort zone of self-composed cool in The Descendants, Alexander Payne's beautifully gentle, funny and moving film.

author: Joe Williams
quote: An Oscar-ready collaboration between a great director and a star at the peak of his powers, but at its heart is a message in a bottle reading: "Trapped in paradise. Please send help."

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: At bottom, all Payne's films make us smile, often ruefully but hopefully.

author: Roger Moore
quote: The Descendants lets Payne show us the Other America and the Other Americans - little lives caught up in small but epic problems far away from the La La Land of Hollywood hype, sex and violence.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The two most moving scenes require extraordinary performances from supporting players...Forster is as deserving of a supporting actor nomination as anyone I have seen this year.

author: Ty Burr
quote: I can't think of another movie this year that made me laugh or weep harder for the whole lumpy business of being - the compromises and connections that get us through the day and somehow add up to entire lives.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Clooney has never been better, subtler, more deeply rooted in a performance than he is in The Descendants. And he's funny, too.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Rarely has a contemporary movie taken in so much life and revealed it with such depth of feeling.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: A pitch-perfect movie that threads a microscopically tiny needle between high comedy and devastating drama.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: It's such a disappointment that The Descendants isn't a better movie than it is. In this soap opera disguised as a comedy, Payne, who was always a master at balancing sharp satire with an essential humanism, has traded his tart lemon center for a squishy marshmallow one.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: The movie itself is a worthy thing, too, but it's not as good as Clooney is here, which is to say, it's not great.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Payne's observational humor and attention to detail yield something emotionally epic. Everything from beachfront jogs to hospital confessions reveals layers of humanity and absurdity.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Expertly mixing tears and laughs with the sort of alchemy not seen since "Terms of Endearment," this superbly written, directed, acted, and yes, Oscar-friendly movie perfectly captures the blackly comic insanity that can overtake a family forced to confront an impending death.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The Descendants is an ultra-polished picture in which every emotion we're supposed to feel has been cued up well in advance. There's nothing surprising or affecting about it. Not even Clooney, who works wonders with the occasional piece of dialogue, can save it.

author: Rex Reed
quote: The Descendants is a soap opera with Hawaiian shirts.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: It's one of the year's most pleasurable American movies.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Alexander Payne has won an Oscar for best adapted screenplay (Sideways), but you'd never guess that from this clumsily written drama: characters keep explaining things that their listeners would already know, and the first couple reels are so thick with expository voice-over that you may think you're listening to a museum tour on a set of headphones.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Clooney and Payne are coconspirators, too. They know that the story they are telling is too emotionally complicated to muck up with a lot of preening and artifice. They head right into the sad and crazymaking humor of the situation. This is a modest marvel of a movie.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Another beautifully chiseled piece of filmmaking - sharp, funny, generous, and moving.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: What happens is that we get vested in the lives of these characters. That's rare in a lot of movies.

author: Noel Murray
quote: The movie suffers from backstory-heavy voiceover narration in its first half, followed by an excess of quirky laugh lines down the stretch, just when it seems to be finding a stronger rhythm. There's a shameless crowd-pleasing element to The Descendants that keeps its harder truths about family relationships at bay.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: It makes The Descendants a tragedy infused with comedy and calls for a balancing act from filmmaker and star alike, a tightrope they navigate with nary a wobble.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Payne's low-key approach only deepens the film's intimate power. Want a movie you can really connect with? The Descendants is damn near perfect.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: To call The Descendants perfect would be a kind of insult, a betrayal of its commitment to, and celebration of, human imperfection. Its flaws are impossible to distinguish from its pleasures.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: This is an exquisite portrait of a family navigating the wreckage imparted to them by one of their own.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: It's a precarious balance, but Payne blends wit and poignancy so artfully it feels like an exquisitely choreographed dance.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: It left me cold. The pathos is as unearned as the protagonist's privilege.

author: David Edelstein
