quote: Payne is too acerbic - maybe too much of an asshole - to settle for easy humanism. But he's too smart a dramatist to settle for easy derision. Mockery and empathy seesaw, the balance precarious - and thrillingly so. It's the noblest kind of satire: cruel and yet, in the end, lacking the killing blow.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: In short, The Descendants is the latest exhibit in Payne's careful dissection of the beached male, which runs from Matthew Broderick's character in "Election" to Jack Nicholson's in "About Schmidt" and Paul Giamatti's in "Sideways."

author: Eric Kohn
quote: The Descendants constantly hovers on the brink of a dark comedy. But it never takes the big plug. By treading carefully, Payne has created his warmest, most earnest work, if not his best.

author: Gail MacDonald
quote: Heartwarming, tragic and, at times, hilariously funny drama.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: After a five-year wait since "Sideways," Alexander Payne has made his best film yet with The Descendants. Ostensibly a study of loss and coping with a tragic situation, this wonderfully nuanced look at a father and two daughters dealing with the imminent death of his wife and their mother turns the miraculous trick of possibly being even funnier than it is moving.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: Nearly every detail sources directly back to Kaui Hart Hemmings' sensitively crafted novel, and yet, Payne's triumph is in striking the right tone -- and knowing what to leave unsaid.

author: Jaime N. Christley
quote: In a development that seemed to begin in earnest with "Sideways," a large part of The Descendents seems to operate on a non-narrative level.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: The Descendants is that rare bird, moving, enlightening, funny and unapologetically human. It's one of the year's best pictures, one to savor and think about.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: The Descendants is gentle, witty, audience-friendly entertainment for grown-ups, with a great performance by one of our biggest screen stars.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: I'm a notorious softie, and I found things to like about the film, most particularly Clooney's performance; but I remained untouched.

author: Angie Errigo
quote: It's just too tempting to dismiss it as extremely long and incredibly disappointing. It's challenging, divisive and has moments of beauty but leaves you cold.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: This precious story line, adapted from a novel by Jonathan Safran Foer, keeps shriveling up against the backdrop of a traumatized city; only gaunt Max von Sydow, as a mute old man who accompanies the young hero on his rounds, supplies the grave authority the premise demands.

author: Mike Scott
quote: All along, though, I was struck by an even stronger feeling, that I was sitting in on somebody else's therapy session. That's not a comfortable feeling -- and that makes Extremely Loud & Incredibly Close considerably less rewarding than it should be.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close is supposed to promote healing, but as they say in New York: close, but no cigar.

author: Steve Persall
quote: The terror of Sept. 11 feels like little more than a dramatic hook, an easy way to make audiences cry. Oskar and the event defining him deserve better.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: You need lots of gifted people chasing after the same bad idea to make a movie as colossally misguided as Extremely Loud & Incredibly Close.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: As a portrait of what happens to a family when its glue disappears, Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close wrung a bucket of tears out of me.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: He (Horn) gets so deeply into the whirling mind of Oskar Schell, dominating every scene he's in – which is almost every scene, period – that he lifts the movie out of the realm of "Forrest Gump"-like emotional manipulation.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: If actors this good cannot overcome their material, then we can only say: Tom Hanks, Sandra Bullock … Max von Sydow, Zoe Caldwell, Viola Davis, Jeffrey Wright, John Goodman… thanks for your honest efforts in the service of a fundamentally dishonest weepie.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: In supporting roles, Bullock and Hanks deliver performances that are low-key and perfectly scaled. Viola Davis and Jeffrey Wright are, likewise, excellent as a couple Oskar meets on his reconnaissance expedition.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: So all the handsome shots that turn the city into a toyland and all the superb editing and vibrant art direction - all the formal tricks Daldry uses to whip you up and work you over - risk being too much. After 45 minutes, it can feel like junk on a sundae. But the movie has a human coup.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: The quality of the craft at the best moments of the film is undeniable.  But it depends, finally, to how well you can embrace a young man named Horn -- a terrific gamble for a film and a subject of such size.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: It's a far better thing to remember Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close than to watch it. Looking back, much of what is irritating, precious and tiresome about the movie recedes and drops away, while all the movie's virtues, which are considerable, rise to consciousness. There are good things here - just be prepared to blast for them.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: There's a fine line between precocious and insufferable, and it's a line continually crossed by Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: Luckily, Horn is so good -- as is Max von Sydow, in a wordless role -- that the film resonates in spite of the tear-jerking strings Daldry pulls.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: No movie has ever been able to provide a catharsis for the Holocaust, and I suspect none will ever be able to provide one for 9/11. Such subjects overwhelm art.

author: David Denby
quote: Much of what Oskar says in the book is amusingly beside the point. Onscreen, however, the sound of a hyper-articulate boy talking semi-nonsense becomes very hard to take.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Solidly crafted, impeccably acted and self-important in the way that Oscar loves, Extremely Loud is also incredibly close to exploitation.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The only bright spot in Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close is Max von Sydow, as a mysterious, and mysteriously mute.

author: Eric Kohn
quote: If "Extremely Loud" came out in the weeks or months following 9/11, more audiences (and critics) might find an excuse to appreciate the way its soul-searching protagonist works through his grief. Ten years later, his struggle actually feels outrageously old-fashioned.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Daldry and his screenwriter Eric Roth make the mistake of showing bodies falling from the Twin Towers – it's a mistake because its graphic power seems more exploitative than cathartic – but they otherwise thankfully refrain from pulling out all the stops.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: Director Stephen Daldry has taken great care in looking at it through the eyes of a precocious New York City boy in a film filled with both sentiment and substance.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: Renders Jonathan Safran Foer's best-selling 2005 novel into unconvincing Hollywood mush.

author: David Edelstein
quote: But the question hangs: Does this artificial, three-hankie scenario justify its 9/11 appropriations? Dry your eyes and decide for yourself.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Yet these are precisely the sort of pictures that divide audiences over a central question: Are those strings being honestly played or just shamefully pulled? Of course, the answer determines whether you feel moved or merely manipulated.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: About as artistically profound as those framed 3-D photos of the Twin Towers emblazoned with "Never Forget'' that are still for sale in Times Square a decade after 9/11.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: It will always be "too soon" for Extremely Loud And Incredibly Close, which processes the immense grief of a city and a family through a conceit so nauseatingly precious that it's somehow both too literary and too sentimental, cloying yet aestheticized within an inch of its life.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: The result is that, as with Hanks' performance, what's missing - subtlety, truth, an earned sense of rebirth – is stronger than what's here. Despite all the connections in Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close, it never connects to us the way we need it to.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Director Stephen Daldry has fashioned an emotionally powerful cinematic testimony about that horrific late summer day.

author: Mary Pols
quote: Dodging the twin minefields of preciousness and an exploitative 9/11 premise, Horn races away with the movie and makes it believably, genuinely sad.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Yes, you may cry, but when tears are milked as they are here, the truer response should be rage.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: The production's penchant for contrivance is insufferable - not a single spontaneous moment from start to finish - and the boy is so precocious you want to strangle him. It's surely not the fault of Thomas Horn, the remarkable young man who plays him.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: In Extremely Loud & Incredibly Close, director Stephen Daldry must walk a tricky line between poignancy and pathos. He occasionally slips into maudlin turf.

author: R. Kurt Osenlund
quote: This film buries its soul beneath its own pretentious rubble, and the youthful, labyrinthine mind in which it places viewers feels less like an offbeat vehicle for healing than it does a kaleidoscopic prison.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: A polarizing load of quirkiness in Extremely Loud gunks up (at least for this hometown mourner; your results may vary) what is at heart a piercing story.

author: Nick Pinkerton
quote: Such an abundance of "epiphanies," one after another, amount to a tactical assault on viewer sentiments. The deluge of tears is Daldry's idea of pathos, but to these eyes, it's Oscar-trolling 9/11 kitsch.

author: Rex Reed
quote: Certainly not a bad movie, but a disappointing one. It knocks itself out trying to break your heart, but it's too starched and blow-dried for its own good. Maybe if it had manipulated me less, it would have moved me more.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: We might have all felt like lost children for a while, but ten years later, the innocence is shameless.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: It's an emotional powerhouse of a film, an unforgettable and rewarding motion picture experience.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Best of all, von Sydow is absolutely wonderful, with the great veteran actor clearly relishing this very unusual role as he darts, skulks and, in a stealthy way, mugs across town. Without saying a thing, he dominates the middle part of the movie.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: With its re-enactments of that fateful day, Extremely Loud plays a bit too much like one of those perfectly lit, heart-tugging segments TV networks air during the Olympics. It hardly matters that Horn manages to give such a naturalistic, unmannered performance as the young Oskar when everything around him has been so deliberately orchestrated to provoke a specific reaction.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The Help's most apparent flaw is a tendency to paint with broad strokes. With only a few exceptions, it avoids shades of gray.

author: Anna Smith
quote: A simplistic portrayal of historic race relations boosted by terrific performances from some of the best actresses working in Hollywood today. Sure, it's corny, but it mostly works.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: Big hair, fine period frocks and interior design lend The Help a pleasingly retro look. Yet for someone who grew up in Mississippi, the director has little sense of place.

author: Ben Sachs
quote: As in many reductive period pieces, there are no real characters here, just archetypes, namely reactionary cretins and sensitive souls who anticipate modern attitudes.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Instead of plunging us into a racist past, however, The Help takes us on a pop-cultural tour that savors the picturesque, and strengthens stereotypes it purports to shatter.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: The Help is a high-functioning tearjerker, but the catharsis it offers feels glib and insufficient, a Barbie Band-Aid on the still-raw wound of race relations in America.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The "black maid" may be a cliché. But when was the last time we saw a story told from her point of view?

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: Emma Stone, for example, is no one's idea of an ugly duckling. And though she offers a sincere effort, she never quite settles into the role of Skeeter.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: Spencer, a superb performer mainly known for small character parts, gives a star-making turn as the won't-take-no-guff Minny.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: The Help has a saucy, humorous side.

author: Connie Ogle
quote: The Help will make you laugh, yes, but it can also break your heart. In the dog days of August moviegoing, that's a powerful recommendation.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: The whole thing is rigged for crowd-pleasing payoffs - a bit about chocolate pie gets more mileage than a Prius - and those payoffs are about honoring white viewers for not being horrible racists. Kudos to them.

author: David Edelstein
quote: It's a tough, beautifully judged performance (Davis) - it gives this too-soft movie a spine.

author: Mary Pols
quote: For every obvious turn The Help takes, there is Davis, the ideal counterweight.

author: Steve Persall
quote: It's a movie of terrific performances and rousing comeuppances, with a side order of corn pone for the soul.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: The Help may be more interested in the moral at the end of the story than the story itself, but what saves the film from its meticulous one-dimensionality is that nuanced, deeply moving cast.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: The movie is too pious for farce and too eager to please to comment persuasively on the racial horrors of the Deep South at that time.

author: Andrew Schenker
quote: High school creative-writing-class ironies of all kinds abound in The Help.

author: Sara Maria Vizcarrondo
quote: A chick flick for do-gooders, The Help suffers from a malady common to the discrimination drama: its treatment of inequality is more condescending than the prejudice it aims to remedy.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Davis is reason No. 1 the film extracted from Kathryn Stockett's 2009 best-seller improves on its source material.

author: Peter Travers
quote: A deeply touching human story filled with humor and heartbreak is rare in any movie season, especially summer. That's what makes The Help an exhilarating gift.

author: Joe Williams
quote: It's a credit to the cast and to the worthiness of the idea that this overlong movie works at all. But those of us who already know that racism is bad could use a little more challenge and a little less help.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Save for Ms. Davis's, however, the performances are almost all overly broad, sometimes excruciatingly so, characterized by loud laughs, bugging eyes and pumping limbs.

author: Karina Longworth
quote: We get a fairly typical Hollywood flattening of history, with powerful villains and disenfranchised heroes.

author: Meghan Keane
quote: If you can suspend your disbelief that a cute 22 year-old had the power to succeed with civil rights where Martin Luther King and President Kennedy failed, The Help actually has a lot to offer.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: This is a good film, involving and wonderfully acted. I was drawn into the characters and quite moved, even though all the while I was aware it was a feel-good fable, a story that deals with pain but doesn't care to be that painful.

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: Laughter, which is ladled on thick as gravy, proves to be the secret ingredient - turning what should be a feel-bad movie about those troubled times into a heart-warming surprise.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Like its characters, it has its faults. But overall, it is a movie of imaginative sympathy that gets into the skin of its characters, into their hearts, and, ultimately, into ours.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: The Help definitely worked on me as a consummate tear-jerker with a terrific cast, and it's pretty much the summer's only decent Hollywood drama.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: Good story, great characters, a setting plucked from history - and a multiracial, multigenerational ensemble cast stacked with fabulous actresses. But the thing that makes The Help such a rousing crowd-pleaser is its generous helping of baked goods.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The Help sidesteps easy sentimentality. As the film's heart and soul, Davis and Spencer add vast reserves of depth and dignity to a crowd-pleasing tale.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: As affectionately as Taylor has brought The Help to the screen, and as gratifying as it is to watch Davis and Spencer bring Aibileen and Minny to palpable, fully rounded life, their narrative, like "The Blind Side" a few years ago, is structured largely around their white female benefactor.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: It's a carefully manicured, almost genteel piece of moviemaking. The film is paradoxically both rousing and lulling.

author: Mike Scott
quote: The Help isn't intended to be so much a movie about the ugliness of the era than an optimistic tale of what can spring from that kind of ugliness, about the ability of people to love one another even when they're surrounded by hatred. And on that level, The Help succeeds wonderfully, a warm and sweet song of hope.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Typically, this sort of film is an earnest tear-jerker with moments of levity. Instead, what we have here is a raucous rib-tickler with occasional pauses for a little dramatic relief.

author: Sara Stewart
quote: Director Tate Taylor is a childhood friend of Stockett and hasn't done much else, which may be why The Help feels clumsy but well-intentioned.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Davis and Spencer give faces and fully-fleshed out lives to women who must have been more than what they did for a living as The Help.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: Taylor does capture the Jim Crow era and its anxieties well, but his characters tend toward the facile and his white heroine is too idealized.

author: David Denby
quote: The Help is, in some way, crude and obvious, but it opens up a broad new swath of experience on the screen, and parts of it are so moving and well acted that any objections to what's second-rate seem to matter less as the movie goes on.  [15 & 22 August 2011, p. 96]

author: Peter Debruge
quote: A stirring black-empowerment tale aimed squarely at white audiences, The Help personalizes the civil rights movement through the testimony of domestic servants working in Jackson, Miss., circa 1963.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: The acting is uniformly excellent, and the cause - dragging the beginnings of civil rights into Jackson, Miss., at great risk - couldn't be nobler. What the film lacks is a strong point of view.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Although a nip and a tuck here and there might improve Hugo's overall pace, there is no denying that this love letter to the movies is something to cherish.

author: David Edelstein
quote: For all the wizardry on display, Hugo often feels like a film about magic instead of a magical film.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Visually Hugo is a marvel, but dramatically it's a clockwork lemon.

author: Kim Newman
quote: This is a great director's greatest love story.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: With Hugo, Martin Scorsese has accomplished what few in Hollywood are willing to try: make a movie for adults that arrives without sex, violence, or profanity and earns a PG-rating.

author: Andrew Lapin
quote: There is much to observe, for Hugo (the film) is a marvel of spectacle, a sensory feast steeped in cinematic lore that proves pure joy is attainable in three dimensions.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Hugo states, in its adamant, straightforward poetry, that old things do matter.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Hugo is Scorsese's most personal film, from the standpoint of both an artist and a grandfather. He is as interested in Melies' posterity as in making a movie that his descendants can see before they're adults.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Scorsese transforms this innocent tale into an ardent love letter to the cinema and a moving plea for film preservation.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Hugo is a mixed bag but one well worth rummaging through.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: Bursting with earned emotion, Hugo is a mechanism that comes to life at the turn of a key in the shape of a heart.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: Young Hugo (Asa Butterfield), a boy who literally lives inside the clocks he manages in a grand Paris train station in the 1930s, embodies one problem that bedeviled even Dickens: He's boringly nice.

author: Mike Scott
quote: This kind of cinematic delight is a rarity, a warm and masterfully crafted reminder of why we love to go to the movies in the first place.

author: Steven Rea
quote: What about the kids and families who have no connection to Méliès, little familiarity with Charlie Chaplin or Buster Keaton? Will Hugo keep them in their seats? I'm not sure.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: The performances are universally good, the 3-D is utterly gorgeous, and the nutshell history of the early days of movies is inspiring.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: As well as an engaging fable about a homeless orphan living in a train station, Scorsese's film is a richly illustrated lesson in cinema history and the best argument for 3-D since James Cameron's "Avatar."

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Somehow, for me, this earnest, pretty movie never came to life on screen; it remained a curio in a cabinet, to be admired through a pane of glass.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: Instead of sticking with the familiar, Scorsese has followed his impulses into something that feels entirely new but is still distinctively his. He has made a potential holiday classic, an exciting, comic and sentimental melodrama that will satisfy children and adults alike and reward repeat viewings for many years to come.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: As much of a personal Scorsese picture as "Raging Bull" or "Taxi Driver." In some ways, this could be his most heartfelt movie.

author: Phil Coldiron
quote:
Scorsese's affection for cinema is, of course, no surprise, and Hugo doesn't
shy away from stumping for the cause of his Film Foundation; which isn't to say it's a vanity project, at least not any more than any film with a budget in the nine figures is.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: What Scorsese has really made is a beautifully crafted love letter to movies, the passion of his life. What sounded like an odd pairing winds up being a perfect fit.

author: Joe Williams
quote: For cinematic sojourners, Hugo is a trip to the moon.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Rich and stimulating even when it wanders.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: All the actors are wonderful, including Sacha Baron Cohen as a villainous Inspector.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: The way Hugo deals with Melies is enchanting in itself, but the film's first half is devoted to the escapades of its young hero. In the way the film uses CGI and other techniques to create the train station and the city, the movie is breathtaking.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: You still can't help admiring the project's ambition; an odd combo of "Babe: Pig in the City" and Godard's "Histoire(s) du cinéma," Hugo is the strangest bird to grace the multiplex in a while.

author: Ty Burr
quote: An exhilarating tale of magic, machines, memories, and dreams, Martin Scorsese pulls off the neatest trick of all. He marshals the marvels of modern movie technology - up to and including the dreaded 3-D - to create a love letter to the earliest of movies and, by extension, to every movie from then to now.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: The result is a movie that's kinetic yet slow, whose joys are architectural more than spiritual.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: It's a complex fusion of film history and personal history, filled with dazzling embellishments and unabashed sentiment about the glories of cinema.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: Waves of melancholy wash over the story and keep the treacle at bay, as do the spasms of broad comedy, much of it nimbly executed by Mr. Baron Cohen.

author: Karina Longworth
quote: Perfunctorily mounted as a children's adventure, Hugo is weirdly staid in its pacing, and the screenplay, by Scorsese's "Aviator" collaborator John Logan, is full of groaners. The movie is far more successful as a barely veiled issue flick.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Hugo both ticks and flies by, a marvel meant to be pulled from the cabinet and enjoyed again and again.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: The director's visually thrilling Hugo has real moments of 3-D magic. Sadly, they aren't quite enough to make this adaptation of Brian Selznick's celebrated novel, "The Invention of Hugo Cabret," a wholly satisfying experience.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Strangely, Scorsese's very passion for the subject matter turns out to be both a blessing and a curse for Hugo.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Scorsese builds Hugo in the Méliès manner, creating a complete, ravishing Parisian world on a soundstage in England and reveling in the sheer transporting joy of it. Hugo will take your breath away.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: Magical and imaginative, this eye-popping masterpiece from director Martin Scorsese will transport audiences to a place they won't believe.

author: Roger Moore
quote: A stunning exercise in 3D and a delightful celebration of Scorsese's lifelong love of the movies, something he, like Hugo, developed on childhood.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: Overall, however, the manner in which the film blends the tale of an imperiled boy and the history of cinema makes for an ambitious and fanciful ride.

author: David Denby
quote: Hugo is superbly playful.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: In attempting to make his first film for all ages, Martin Scorsese has fashioned one for the ages. Simultaneously classical and modern, populist but also unapologetically personal, Hugo flagrantly defies the mind-numbing quality of most contempo kidpics.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: A fabulous and passionate love letter to the cinema and its preservation framed by the strenuous adventures of two orphans in 1930s Paris.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: A hymn to that beautiful city, is among his least consequential efforts. It's attractive and easy to slip into, but he didn't put enough thought into the design, and it soon falls apart.

author: Mike Scott
quote: A lovely jaunt that ends up becoming one of Allen's most enjoyable films, start-to-finish, in years.

author: Joe Williams
quote: A fanciful French cousin to Allen's "Zelig" and "The Purple Rose of Cairo," yet the fulfilled wish for a better life is high-concept absurdity without high-anxiety guffaws.

author: Kimberley Jones
quote: Quite simply, Midnight in Paris is charming – très charmant, to ape the argot of the locals. I say that somewhat tongue-in-cheek, as this is very much an outsider's valentine to the City of Lights.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Allen eventually gets to the heart of this matter: the allure and danger of nostalgia.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: Midnight in Paris initially seems like a departure for Allen, but the prevailing theme blends right in with the rest of his canon.

author: Carrie Rickey
quote: Midnight in Paris is not a perfect movie - as in "Julie & Julia" one senses its creator's impatience to leave the bleached-out present for the colorful past. But it is warm and effortless, qualities that make it embraceable.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Allen's filmmaking technique isn't what it once was, true.  But at age 75 he still manages to keep a spry pace going even if something less than impeccable craft hobbles the photography and editing.

author: Rick Groen
quote: Certainly, his (Allen) work here feels effortless, and that feather-light touch gives the picture its charm – modest but real.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Souffle-light and long on charm.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: But lo! Isn't that Owen Wilson, blond and goyische to the gills, yet faithfully replicating the put-upon slump of the Allen shoulders, the quavering stammers about art vs. success, literature vs. Hollywood?

author: Ty Burr
quote: A sweet-natured trifle, as flavorful and as thin as a crepe.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Sheer pleasure to watch, full of rich visuals and felicitous comic turns.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: A movie that's loving and wistful and often hysterically funny.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: Woody Allen's bad movies often seem to be taking place in some kind of upper-class fantasy world, which may be the reason I find this upfront fantasy to be his funniest, most agreeable comedy in years.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Led by Wilson and Cotillard, the ensemble makes the most of the material that works, and makes the best of the rest of it.

author: Kerry Lengel
quote: A delicious trifle for anyone who has ever dreamt of bantering about the cinema with Luis Buñuel or lounging at the piano to hear Cole Porter sing "Let's Do It."

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Owen Wilson is a key to the movie's appeal. He makes Gil so sincere, so enthusiastic.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: A sweet, not altogether satisfying variation on the fantasy-becomes-reality conceit he (Allen) used in his Depression-era "The Purple Rose of Cairo."

author: Dana Stevens
quote: A trifle in both senses of the word: a feather-light, disposable thing, and a rich dessert appealingly layered with cake, jam, and cream.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: As in "Purple Rose," the film works best when tweaking the disparate worlds thrown together, though "Midnight" is frothier, and so Wilson shines.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: Owen Wilson turns out to be the best Woody Allen surrogate by far.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The best Allen movie in 10 years, or maybe even close to 20 - is all about that idea: Reckoning with the past as a real place, but also worrying about the limits of nostalgia.

author: Peter Travers
quote: What's fresh about Midnight in Paris is the way he (Allen) identifies with Gil's idealization of the past, of the Paris that represented art and life at their fullest.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: An unassuming wisp of a movie, Midnight In Paris finds Woody Allen penning a love letter to the City Of Lights, albeit one whose sentiments could easily fit on a postcard.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: With Midnight in Paris, Allen has lightened up, allowed himself a treat and in the process created a gift for us and him.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: It is marvelously romantic, even though - or precisely because - it acknowledges the disappointment that shadows every genuine expression of romanticism.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: In Woody Allen's beguiling and then bedazzling new comedy, nostalgia isn't at all what it used to be - it's smarter, sweeter, fizzier and ever so much funnier.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The film is constructed in such a way that suspension of disbelief isn't too hard because there's something universal in what Allen explores. Like Santa Claus, we know it's not real but the idea is so appealing that we go along with the fantasy.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The familiar dialogue here makes one long for something closer to the edginess of "Manhattan" or the offbeat humor of "Vicky Cristina Barcelona."

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Allen has fun in his imaginary French capital, turning his star-studded cast loose to interpret their characters as they wish.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: This is prime Woody Allen - insightful, philosophical and very funny.

author: Karina Longworth
quote: The latest in a long line of actors playing a "Woody Allen type" in a Woody Allen film, Wilson bends his own recognizably nasal Texan drawl into an exaggerated pattern of staccatos and glissandos that's obviously modeled on the writer/director's near-musical verbal cadences.

author: Rex Reed
quote: In a film so ripe with temptations for posturing, exaggeration and satirical overacting, nobody is anything less than natural, unpretentious and funny as hell.

author: David Edelstein
quote: This supernatural comedy isn't just Allen's best film in more than a decade; it's the only one that manages to rise above its tidy parable structure and be easy, graceful, and glancingly funny, as if buoyed by its befuddled hero's enchantment.

author: David Denby
quote: Midnight has one big problem: Allen hardly gives Gil a perceptive moment. He's awestruck and fumbling - he doesn't possess, to our eyes, the conviction of a writer. But who knows? He's young.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: Allen seems to be paying attention in a way he hasn't always done in recent films, and has found a way to channel his often-caustic misanthropy, half-comic fear of death and anti-American bitterness into agreeable comic whimsy.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: Woody Allen's time-travelling comedy Midnight In Paris is a valentine to Paris and an absolute delight.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Darius Khondji's cinematography evokes to the hilt the gorgeously inviting Paris of so many people's imaginations (while conveniently ignoring the rest), and the film has the concision and snappy pace of Allen's best work.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: Like a swoony lost chapter from "Paris, je t'aime" agreeably extended to feature length.

author: Olly Richards
quote: You don't need to understand anything of baseball to get behind this, a chest-swelling story about second chances and flipping a finger up (even a giant foam one) to The Man.

author: David Denby
quote: Statistics and their alleged true meaning are at the heart of Moneyball, but it's also one of the most soulful of baseball movies - it confronts the anguish of a tough game.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: A crowd-pleasing baseball movie for people - like me - who don't like baseball movies...Probably the finest baseball movie since "Bull Durham".

author: Mike Scott
quote: Pitt and Hill are fantastic individually, and hilarious when together -- and on a surprisingly engaging script by Aaron Sorkin ("Social Network") and Steve Zaillian ("Schindler's List").

author: Rick Groen
quote: Turning the stately game into something few can resist – a smart and lively comedy of manners.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Moneyball presents a misleading story line in order to prop up Billy Beane as some kind of would-be miracle worker antihero. In truth, he's just another tobacco-chewing go-getter trying to make sense of a game that, thankfully, has never quite made sense.

author: Bill Weber
quote: True to Hollywood's tireless efforts to fit square-peg material into roundish genre niches, this wavering, intermittently smart story of daring to think differently flattens its narrative into formula.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Moneyball is one of the best and most viscerally exciting films of the year.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: On some level Moneyball is about loyalty: loyalty to an idea, loyalty to a partnership forged by desperation, loyalty to the values you believe in. Whether that was Lewis' intention in the book, or Beane's intention in taking the risk, doesn't matter. It's the formula Miller came up with for the film, and with the team of Pitt and Hill, it's a winning one.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Moneyball is a smart, funny, and thoughtful baseball movie.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: It's tense, strangely funny in a lot of spots and – if you grew up loving old-fashioned, seat-of-the-pants baseball, as I did – the most depressing movie of the year.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: The movie is an absolute triumph of culturally relevant filmmaking – a film that will thrill and fascinate sport junkies and non-fans alike. If you like baseball, you will love this movie. If you hate baseball, you will still love this movie.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: The real protagonist of Moneyball, however, is Beane himself, played with great charisma by Brad Pitt. (With this movie and "The Tree of Life" competing against each other, Pitt could wind up cheating himself out of an Oscar this year.)

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Moneyball is the perfect sports movie for these cash-strapped times of efficiency maximization.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: Now that Pitt no longer has brash youth on his side, he's digging deeper and doing more with less. It's the kind of acting - understated but woven with golden threads of movie-star style - that gives us more to look at rather than less.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Although you don't have to be a sports fan to enjoy it, Moneyball is one of the best baseball movies imaginable.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Funny, furious, and full of front-office drama.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: His performance is a canny portrait of leadership - part genius, part crazy guts, part dumb luck - and worthy of moving Pitt up to the playoff round of Oscar finalists for Best Actor. We'd put money on it.

author: Ty Burr
quote: Moneyball is a hilarious and provocative change-up, entertaining without feeling the need to swing for the fences.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: This extraordinary hybrid of a movie lives and breathes the game, yet its achievement is bigger than that. There's a touch of old-fashioned romanticism here, but more crucially there's strategy going on inside Bennett Miller's movie that turns it into something cool and special.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: It's hard to imagine anyone but Mr. Pitt in the role. He's relaxed yet edgy and sometimes unsettling.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Never before, though, have statistics added up to such electrifying entertainment. After the mostly minor-league productions of recent months, this movie, which was directed by Bennett Miller, renews your belief in the power of movies.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Starring Brad Pitt in top movie star form, it's a film that's impressive and surprising.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: The supporting cast is strong, as is the deft, sharply witty script. Miller directs elegantly, letting the narrative unfold at a deliberate, artful pace.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: It's a sports story, yes, because without baseball there's no Beane.  But it's far more a tale of a man's triumph over himself and his doubters.  And you don't need math to make sense of that.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Moneyball comes to life when elaborating on Beane's unique system of player selection, and the on-field baseball action is at times electrifying, but it trends toward the generic when tailing him away from the stadium.

author: Peter Hartlaub
quote: Naysayers have been claiming for years that the "Moneyball" book wouldn't work as a movie. But ultimately, it's the cinematic touches that keep this film version from becoming something exceptional.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Like a cold beer under a bluebird sky; like a flawless line drive on a warm summer's day; like a long, languorous seventh-inning stretch - Moneyball satisfies.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: A richly detailed and enjoyable American yarn.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: It's to the director's credit, and Pitt's, that Moneyball is anything but bloodless - in its own quiet, unspectacular way, this movie courses with life.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Moneyball is a thinking person's baseball movie, and a baseball fan's thinking movie.

author: Rex Reed
quote: This is a subtle, elegant and altogether triumphant film about a subject I thought I was tired of, told with an artistry and freshness that is positively thrilling.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: The supersmart and rousing Moneyball, which may be the best baseball movie since "Bull Durham," is also about talk, but in a coolly heady and original inside-the-front-office way.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: Miller directs with intelligence, though not flair, but the script makes up for any flagging energy with crackling Sorkin dialogue and performances that sing with revolutionary fervor.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: A smart, intense and moving film that isn't so much about sports as about the war between intuition and statistics. I walked in knowing what the movie was about, but unprepared for its intelligence and depth.

author: Stephen Garrett
quote: Best of all, filmmaker Bennett Miller (Capote) uses this brainiac sports movie to remind viewers that money is neither the measure of a man nor the ultimate assessment of quality; it's a myopic metric based on past accomplishments rather than future potential. After all, success isn't always about the home runs so much as just getting on base - again, and again, and again.

author: Robert Wilonsky
quote: It really happened, it's really corny, and it's really great.

author: David Edelstein
quote: The movie doesn't quite come together, but it's full of smart, cynical talk, and it's very entertaining.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: The surprisingly effective Moneyball has a smart script, solid direction and great performances.

author: Eric Kohn
quote: Director Bennett Miller has produced a warm and generally agreeable character study about the pratfalls of athletic institutions and the willingness to think outside the box.

author: Kirk Honeycutt
quote: The movie does achieve something nearly impossible: Someone who doesn't even like the sport may care about Billy Beane and the 2002 Oakland Athletics.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: While a hopelessly awkward-looking Hill provides fish-out-of-water laughs, Pitt gives a genuinely soul-searching performance.

author: Lee Cassanell
quote: You don't have to like a piece of art to appreciate the artists vision. Terrence Malick has created a beautiful and ambitious meditation on memory, childhood and the nature of being.

author: Jonathan Crocker
quote: Much of The Tree Of Life’s beauty is in its yearning and wonder. It’s an extraordinary grasping stretch – across space and time – to touch what will always be just out of our reach. It’s a captivating, unmissable experience.

author: Peter Bradshaw
quote: This is visionary cinema on an unashamedly huge scale: cinema that's thinking big. Malick makes an awful lot of other film-makers look timid and negligible by comparison.

author: Kevin Jagernauth
quote: With The Tree Of Life the director has once again created a cinematic experience that is uniquely his own, often powerful and mesmerizing, at times overreaching and overbearing, but never forgettable.

author: Lee Marshall
quote: A cinematic symphony more than a classic narrative film, Terrence Malick’s long-awaited The Tree Of Life has moments of breathtaking visual and aural beauty, but in the end it has us longing for the days of Badlands, Days Of Heaven or The Thin Red Line, when the Texan auteur also knew how to spin a good yarn.

author: Dave Calhoun
quote: While it fascinates as much as it frustrates, the film’s saving grace is that it always feels honest and never cynical. It seems both relevant to us and personal to the filmmaker.

author: Moira Macdonald
quote: Malick, director of photography Emmanuel Lubezki and the cast create a mood that lifts the viewer through the occasional head-scratching moments and into a place of serenity, where answers somehow seem in reach.

author: Ian Nathan
quote: There is simply nothing like it out there: profound, idiosyncratic, complex, sincere and magical; a confirmation that cinema can aspire to art.

author: Rick Groen
quote: The result actually plays like a divine pronouncement, cosmic in scope and oracular in tone, a cinematic sermon on the mount that shows its creator in exquisite form.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Ponderous and perplexing, a somberly audacious film to make viewers swoon or snore, take your pick. It is defiantly opaque, a free-form meditation on nature and nurture across millennia with a tinge of biblical grace.

author: Mike Scott
quote: This film is undoubtedly a piece of art, as much so as a Picasso painting, one that invites viewers to immerse themselves, scratch their heads and consider it.

author: Roger Moore
quote: Glibly put, this challenging time-skipping rumination is the big screen equivalent of watching that "Tree" grow.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Like all his movies except "Badlands," a taut 1973 debut, "Tree" looks gorgeous, has philosophic ambitions, meanders wherever Malick's imagination takes him and stays dramatically inert.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: The movie plays out as a series of memories, so exact and evocative that watching it becomes an immersive experience.

author: Joe Williams
quote: The Tree of Life is a religious experience. Overtly. Audaciously. Unashamedly. No film has ever reached as high toward the face of God and, in our commodified future, few are likely to try.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: The Tree of Life falls short of masterful but retains a power that far too many motion pictures lack. It's about SOMETHING and, even when it fails, it does so in a manner that is interesting and not infantile.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: Beautiful, baffling, poetic, pretentious, it's one big ball of moviedom. Malick tackles the whole shooting match, pondering (and showing) the creation of the universe, life itself, death and the afterlife, and everything in between.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Still, somehow, The Tree of Life - impressionistic, revelatory, elliptical - works.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: An extraordinary thing, and one that I shall likely esteem for a long time.  Philosophically, though, it's still mired in the primordial ooze in a way that will, I suspect, forever make me hold it at arm's length.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: The movie is church via the planetarium. It's as if Malick set out to paint the Sistine Chapel and settled for a dome at the Museum of Natural History.

author: J.R. Jones
quote: A sense of reconciliation is Malick's great accomplishment in The Tree of Life, affording us equal wonder at grace and nature alike.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: The only other film I've seen with this boldness of vision is Kubrick's "2001: A Space Odyssey," and it lacked Malick's fierce evocation of human feeling.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: This may be the most overtly Christian mainstream picture since "The Passion of the Christ." Unlike that one, though, Malick's comes with a generosity of spirit large enough to get all sorts of people (including non-believers) thinking about the nature of faith and what it's all about.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Even when The Tree of Life does not achieve the heights for which it aims, it soars boldly and fearlessly.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: At times trying and perplexing, but it also contains some of the most psychologically insightful and ecstatic filmmaking imaginable.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: At its best, The Tree of Life makes the viewer lean forward, eager to enter Malick's own dreamy, poetic consciousness. At worst, it leads to the vague feeling that we're listening to the meanderings of someone who's not sure we're smart enough to keep up.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: The film is gorgeous and abstract, leaping around in time and space, structured in movements and more like a symphony than a conventional narrative.

The Tree of Life's fetching images are like glowing shards of glass, and together they form a grandiose mirror that reflects Malick's impassioned philosophical outlook. It's unquestionably this great filmmaker's most personal work, a revelation of how he came to be, why he creates, and where he feels he's going.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: If I had to give a two-word review of Terrence Malick's The Tree of Life, it would be: "Wow! Huh??"

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Like so much in this astounding, consistently beautiful and challenging movie, the answer depends on what you bring to it. Think of it as the Ultimate Anti-Summer-Blockbuster.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: For all its flaws, The Tree of Life is a stunning exception to the rule that you can safely check your brain at the popcorn counter until after Labor Day. That's enough to place it among the year's best movies, or at least most-talked-about ones.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Daring in concept, occasionally daffy in execution and ultimately unforgettable, Mr. Malick's film offers a heartfelt answer to the question of where we humans belong - with each other, on this planet, bound by love.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: A shape-shifting film, it resembles a poem. At other moments, it is closer to a symphony. Most often, it approximates a fervent prayer.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: While Malick's great ability holds us for a time, it is finally not enough to compensate for a lack of dramatic involvement - those eschatological quandaries tend to overwhelm the story. The Tree of Life, its enormous advantages notwithstanding, ends up a film that demands to be admired but cannot be easily embraced.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: The middle section of the film, in which we follow Jack's childhood in a series of fragmented memories from birth until about the age of 12, is as astonishingly precise a rendering of the way the world looks to a child as I've seen on film.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Like its predecessors (Badlands, Days of Heaven, The Thin Red Line and The New World), Tree delivers truths that don't go down easy. No one with a genuine interest in the potential of film would think of missing it.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: The Tree of Life is gorgeous to look at. It's also a gargantuan work of pretension and cleverly concealed self-absorption masquerading as spiritual exploration.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: In terms of scale, The Tree Of Life recalls the mammoth ambition of Stanley Kubrick's "2001: A Space Odyssey," but it's also more intimate and personal than Malick's previous films, rooted in vivid memories of growing up in '50s Texas.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: With disarming sincerity and daunting formal sophistication The Tree of Life ponders some of the hardest and most persistent questions, the kind that leave adults speechless when children ask them.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: Malick clings to the promise of grace: His vision of the afterlife is a dreamy beach, enhanced by an excellent playlist of fine classical music, and promising the peace that surpasses all understanding. Plus a beautiful sky.

author: Rex Reed
quote: Content to make movies for himself (Malick) that nobody else wants to see as long as he can find someone to foot the bill, he's also an iconoclast searching for significance. So am I, but not 138 minutes worth. Anyone seeking symmetry in this cinematic taffy pull risks emerging from it with a pretzel for a brain.

author: Keith Uhlich
quote: The Tree of Life enthralls right from the start.

author: Nick Pinkerton
quote: Better than a masterpiece - whatever that is - The Tree of Life is an eruption of a movie, something to live with, think, and talk about afterward.

author: David Edelstein
quote: After warming up with "The Thin Red Line" and "The New World," Malick has succeeded in fully creating his own film syntax, his own temporal reality, and lo, it is … kind of goofy. But riveting.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: Tucked away inside the grandeur, though, and enlivened by jump cuts, is a sharp, not unharrowing story of a father and son, and, amid one's exasperation, there is no mistaking Malick's unfailing ability to grab at glories on the fly.

author: Wade Major
quote: Aggressively impressionistic and unapologetically spiritual, Malick's long-gestating meditation on the meaning of life is, if nothing else, a singularly original and deeply personal film - a growing rarity in American cinema.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Brandishing an ambition it's likely no film, including this one, could entirely fulfill, The Tree of Life is nonetheless a singular work, an impressionistic metaphysical inquiry into mankind's place in the grand scheme of things that releases waves of insights amid its narrative imprecisions.

author: Justin Chang
quote: Result is pure-grade art cinema destined primarily for the delectation of Malick partisans and adventurous arthouse-goers.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: The Tree of Life is pretty much nuts overall, a manic hybrid folly with flashes of brilliance. But even if that's true it's a noble crazy, a miraculous William Butler Yeats kind of crazy, alive with passion for art and the world, for all that is lost and not lost and still to come.

author: Eric Kohn
quote: More meditation than movie, Terrence Malick's The Tree of Life is bound to mystify, awe and exasperate in equal measures.

author: Ian Freer
quote: War Horse is bold, exquisite family filmmaking in the grandest Hollywood tradition. Be warned: whether you're a hippophile or not, it's a four-hankie moviegoing experience.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: The result is a film that may stay in the mind's eye longer than it lingers in the heart.

author: David Denby
quote: War Horse is a bland, bizarrely unimaginative piece of work. [2 Jan. 2012, p.79]

author: Steve Persall
quote: War Horse takes time reaching its full emotional gallop with a late sequence combining man, beast and barbed wire. Yet it remains a technically magnificent ride throughout, and a checklist of visual influences from "All Quiet on the Western Front" to "Gone with the Wind."

author: Betsy Sharkey
quote: Has the sweep of a classic John Ford movie, the sentiment of Frank Capra and a spirited steed named Joey who will steal your heart. The film itself is more difficult to love.

author: Stephanie Zacharek
quote: For all its borrowing from old Hollywood, I don't think War Horse is particularly nostalgic. The word I'd use is wistful. It's the largest, most lavish handful of wistfulness money can buy, and sometimes it's too much. Yet it's nice to know that even Steven Spielberg can still wish for something.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: There isn't a moment in the movie where you don't feel Spielberg's passion, and this time, the film is worthy of his enthusiasm. It's a knockout.

author: David Edelstein
quote: Spielberg has been ridiculed for shooting his actors from below against impossibly Spielbergian skies and a denouement that lays the love on copiously. But there's nothing simpleminded about how he uses movie magic, as a spell to dispel nihilism, to save us from the worst of ourselves by summoning up the best.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: It's almost a great war movie in one direction, and almost a piece of irredeemable cheese in the other, and there you have it.

author: Joe Williams
quote: While the PG-13 approach to the most brutally sustained war the world has ever known makes it suitable for mature children, some cynical adults may resent the tug of the reins. Me, I cried like a grandmother.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Sumptuous and schmaltzy, Steven Spielberg's First World War drama, War Horse, is a strange beast of a film.

author: Mike Scott
quote: An entirely fitting Christmas Day release -- filled as it is with magic and talk of miracles -- and easily one of the best films of 2011.

author: Kyle Smith
quote: It's not a knock on Steven Spielberg to say he is history's finest maker of children's movies. His capacity to evoke simplicity, awe, beauty and unconditional love are his genius, and his vision of the children's story War Horse is a gorgeous, majestic fable about a boy who yearns to be reunited with his steed.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: War Horse's primary attraction is not the story of how it makes us feel but its impressive re-creation of the Great War's battlefields and some stunningly beautiful camerawork by cinematographer Janusz Kaminski.

author: Peter Travers
quote: War Horse gets to you. It's one from the heart.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: To create his disarmingly earnest film, Spielberg draws from the past. Its tone is humanistic and its technique classic.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: It's very much an old-time moviegoing experience; the film could have been made in 1940, and that's a compliment.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Spielberg's typically emotive storytelling only comes to the fore in a few of the film's pivotal action scenes, a couple of which are truly spectacular and remind us only all too well of what this film might have been.

author: Ben Sachs
quote: This may seem slick and sentimental, but beneath the surface is a film every bit as morbid as "A.I. Artificial Intelligence," which also used a nonhuman protagonist to contemplate human death on a mass scale.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Take the theatrical flourish away from this story, however, and the story's thinness becomes apparent.

author: Elizabeth Weitzman
quote: While the Tony-winning play based on the same book creates unexpected impact through strikingly inventive puppetry, Spielberg is at a disadvantage in employing such a literal approach. Not even animals as beautiful as these can substitute for human ingenuity and imagination.

author: A.O. Scott
quote: You may find yourself resisting this sentimental pageant of early-20th-century rural English life, replete with verdant fields, muddy tweeds and damp turnips, but my strong advice is to surrender.

author: Ty Burr
quote: War Horse is the best film of the year. The year, unfortunately, is 1942.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Spielberg and his team - composer John Williams, as always, cinematographer Janusz Kaminski, screenwriter Richard Curtis - never forget their mission: to pull at heart strings, jerk some tears.

author: Shawn Levy
quote: Pieces of War Horse that may charm some eyes might well bore others to tears.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: War Horse, despite its excellences, is a supreme demonstration of a director phoning it in.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Spielberg has created an appropriate showcase for the magnificent creature that emerges, one that recalls the great movie horses of yore in a story guaranteed to pluck, grab and wring viewers' hearts, but thankfully not break them.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: There are extraordinary and beautiful things in War Horse, enough of them to make the movie a pleasure and a worthwhile experience, though not enough to trick the eye or get you believing this movie hangs together.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: War Horse will likely take its place alongside beloved family films. But that doesn't mean sitting through it is pure pleasure. It's a long slog at almost 2½ hours, and occasionally it resorts to obvious sentimentality. At times it's hard to escape the sense that we're watching "Saving Private Ryan"-meets-"The Black Stallion."

author: Jaime N. Christley
quote: What ultimately hobbles War Horse is a two-pronged attack, with Spielberg's soft-sell producing an unfortunately dramatic flatness in almost every scene, while an 11th-hour scramble for picture-book catharsis doesn't seem to work either.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: War Horse is bold, not afraid of sentiment and lets out all the stops in magnificently staged action sequences. Its characters are clearly defined and strongly played by charismatic actors. Its message is a universal one.

author: J. Hoberman
quote: Since he's (Spielberg) a director largely incapable of understatement, War Horse is served up with a self-aggrandizing, distracting surplus of Norman Rockwell backlighting, aerial landscape shots designed to out-swoop David Lean's, and an aggravated sense of doggone wonderment amplified by the director's dependence on John Williams's bombastic score.

author: Rex Reed
quote: War Horse is a don't-miss Spielberg classic that reaches true perfection.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: What's missing, then? There's no fiery central performance in the mix (the horse doesn't count), and once Emily Watson's hardscrabble mom is rotated out of the action, you yearn for an anchor.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: In his most painterly film, Spielberg has appropriated the lavish visual palette of John Ford movies: "The Quiet Man" for the rural settings, "The Horse Soldiers" for the war scenes. Boldly emotional, nakedly heartfelt, War Horse will leave only the stoniest hearts untouched.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: For certain movies, the adjectives "formulaic" and "predictable" are complimentary. War Horse is one of them.

author: Amy Nicholson
quote: Director Steven Spielberg doesn't have a steady grip on War Horse's careening tone, but he'll be damned if there's not 15 minutes in there for everyone.

author: Lisa Schwarzbaum
quote: This is a beautifully built, classically framed movie, shot with the unshowy natural expressiveness of a John Ford Western by Spielberg's great cinematographer, Janusz Kaminski.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Whatever its missteps, this is a film that kids, middle-aged adults and grandparents can all see -- together or separately -- and get something out of in their own ways. There are precious few films that fit this description today and hats off to Spielberg for making one.

author: Justin Chang
quote: This beautifully composed picture brings a robust physicality to tried-and-true source material, but falls short of the sustained narrative involvement and emotional drive its resolutely old-fashioned storytelling demands.

author: Donald Clarke
quote: If we were previously in any doubt, Haneke is confirmed as the premiere European director of his generation.

author: Mike Scott
quote: Amour is a far cry from the warm-and-fuzzy version of love that most people are probably looking for on Valentine's Day. This movie is more of a slap than a hug. But reality hurts sometimes - just like love does.

author: Steve Persall
quote: With Amour, it's the rare feeling of watching a masterpiece unfold.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: The superb Trintignant and the Oscar-nominated Riva – who would win, in a just world – embody once-vigorous people in inevitable decline. Yet as another critic has said, the film is sad without being depressing.

author: Calvin Wilson
quote: The story is so masterfully told that one can't help but be enthralled.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Love means being helpmates throughout all of life's stages. Death is part of love's bargain, and Haneke lays this fact bare.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: This is the sort of small, intimate drama about unpleasant subject matter Hollywood rarely deals with, but Haneke isn't worried about turning off his audience, because death is something everyone has in common. It fascinates us, the way it also scares us.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Amour arrives with plaudits and praise. But this is not hype, it is all deserved. This is a masterpiece.

author: Marc Mohan
quote: While what's on screen is unsparing and clinically presented, the underlying, almost invisible humanity and artistry of the film inspire rather than depress.

author: Ty Burr
quote: What happens between two people? Only the chemistry that keeps us from stumbling through the chaos by ourselves. Is that an illusion, too? Amour says it doesn't much matter. There is no dignity in life except love.

author: Rick Groen
quote: More arduously, Riva is obliged to act out the physical decline while still registering a full spectrum of emotions. Remarkably, she does it all, even when reduced to communicating with her eyes alone. Hers is, in every sense of the phrase, a nakedly honest performance.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: Small, sure and stunningly acted, this is a picture of exacting control.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: Amour is a must-see film that not everyone must see, at least right now.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: Riva, meanwhile, is astounding, not just in the way she portrays the physical manifestation of her decline, particularly later in the film, but also earlier, when she knows she is fading and does not wish to do so. The look in her eyes, the sadness in her face, is crushing.

author: Mick LaSalle
quote: Amour is also unforgettable and one of a kind, two hours of torment that, in the end, you will probably not regret.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: An unexpected kind of masterpiece by Haneke, whose films have included the enigmatic "Caché" and the earlier Golden Palm winner "The White Ribbon." We don't expect such unflinching seriousness, such profundity from Haneke.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: In Amour, these two actors show us what love is, what it really looks like, and what it may, at its most secret moments, demand.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: What makes Amour so strong and clear is that it allows Haneke to anatomize his own severity.

author: James Rocchi
quote: Audiences smart and tough enough to seek the film out will have their own reward.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: This may sound depressing and, in a sense it is, but these things are part of life and Haneke conveys them with a simplicity that is heartbreaking.

author: Kevin Jagernauth
quote: Amour is nevertheless the work of a filmmaker who isn't afraid to ask the big questions about human nature, and coming out of Amour it seems the director has hope for us yet.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: Because of its subject matter, and because of the actors, it's impossible to watch this film without being moved. But a martinet is running the show.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: In the quietly devastating Amour, Haneke's cool, dispassionate gaze feels, for the first time, something like love.

author: Eric Kohn
quote: Although not exactly heartwarming, Amour has a more contained vision of human relationships than Haneke's previous films without sacrificing its bleak foundation. It's his most conventional movie about death -- and the most poignant.

author: Ella Taylor
quote: At his provocative best, though - in his brilliant, gorgeous 2009 film "The White Ribbon," a study of the roots of fascism in domestic tyranny, and now in Amour - Haneke implicates us in the full range of human capacity.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: Michael Haneke's French-language Amour, a perfect film about intertwined lives, proceeds at its own pace, and breathes so deeply that it takes your own breath away.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: This is an unforgettable love story set at the close of day, as tragic and beautiful in its way as "Tristan und Isolde," and a portrait of the impossible beauty and fragility of life that will yield new experiences to every viewer and every viewing.

author: Farran Smith Nehme
quote: All great films have imagination; this one also has the sense of experience.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: A perfect storm of a motion picture, with an icy, immaculate director unexpectedly taking on deeply emotional subject matter.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Small victories that turn into defeats, long walks to gain little ground, little wounds that get deeper every day - growing old is a war, and movies rarely go there. Michael Haneke's amazing, dignified Amour is the exception.

author: David Edelstein
quote: Something sacred passes between Trintignant and Riva. The actress's eyes signal deep awareness as the sounds coming out of her mouth become animalistic.

author: Scott Tobias
quote: A director known for the icy classicism and genre subversion of films like "Funny Games" and "Caché," Haneke has a pitilessness that could not be more perfect for Amour, which would collapse at any whiff of sentimentality.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: A masterpiece about life, death and everything in between.

author: Nick Pinkerton
quote: Haneke remains, by his rules, infallible. So what? A movie in which incident is as spare as it is in Amour can certainly be great; a movie in which ideas and feelings are so sparse cannot.

author: Mary Corliss
quote: In the history of movies about love, Amour shall last forever.

author: Deborah Young
quote: Magnificent in its simplicity and its relentless honesty about old age, illness and dying, Michael Haneke's Amour is a deliberately torturous watch.

author: Peter Travers
quote: These two glam stars of French cinema – Riva in 1959's "Hiroshima Mon Amour" and Trintignant in 1966's "A Man and a Woman" – give performances of breathtaking power and beauty. Prepare for an emotional wipeout.

author: Rex Reed
quote: Don't let Amour join the legion of "Best Films You Never Saw." I urge you to share its sweetness and wisdom, and learn something.

author: Alison Willmore
quote: For all that it is, as promised, about love, it's also a subtly punishing affair that grinds you into the ground as you watch an elderly couple deal with one member's slow deterioration of health and sanity.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: It's not an easy sit; we're never let off the hook with golden-hued memories or belated bits of wisdom. Maybe this is love after all.

author: Peter Bradshaw
quote: The icy message may be that love is not a consolation as we face death. Rather the reverse. Love will give your death meaning, but make it no less unbearable.

author: David Hughes
quote: Michael Haneke's Palme D'Or winner is uncomfortable, uncompromising, unflinching... and utterly unmissable. Old age may not be a reality you wish to confront, but you must see this film.

author: Calum Marsh
quote: This isn't the work of a newly moral or humanistic filmmaker, but another ruse by the same unscrupulous showman whose funny games have been beguiling us for years.

author: Jamie Graham
quote: A compassionate, masterful work that deservedly won Haneke a second Palme d'Or after "The White Ribbon's" 2009 victory. Best to avoid on a first date, though.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: Considering Haneke's confrontational past, this poignantly acted, uncommonly tender two-hander makes a doubly powerful statement about man's capacity for dignity and sensitivity when confronted with the inevitable cruelty of nature.

author: Dan Jolin
quote: An old-school espionage thriller with a movie-biz comedy twist, all the better for being (almost) entirely true. It is to Ben Affleck's credit that the tension and laughs complement rather than neutralise each other.

author: Richard Jordan
quote: Combining laughs and thrills with plenty of verve, Ben Affleck continues his smart directorial career with a stylish, gripping hostage drama.

author: Peter Rainer
quote: In some ways the movie might have been better if it had been about those two Hollywood guys with only occasional blips from the hostage crisis in Iran.

author: Mike Scott
quote: Sharp, brisk and highly entertaining.

author: Dana Stevens
quote: Argo isn't quite on the level of the Sidney Lumet classics to which Affleck pays stylistic homage - smart and taut as it is, it lacks the broader political vision of a film like "Dog Day Afternoon." But Lumet lite still goes down pretty smooth.

author: Andrew Schenker
quote: Undeniably rousing, but deeply irresponsible, Argo fans the flames surrounding historical events likely to still remain raw in the memory of many viewers.

author: Michael Phillips
quote: In the populist vein of Ron Howard's "Apollo 13," Affleck's rouser salutes the Americans (and, more offhandedly, the Canadians) who restored our sense of can-do spirit when we needed it.

author: Lawrence Toppman
quote: Among many things that make the taut thriller Argo remarkable is this one: It depicts a 1980 rescue of American hostages from Iran yet begins by pointing out that the United States was partly responsible for the situation.

author: Joe Neumaier
quote: Argo is movie magic. Ben Affleck's third directorial outing, is an entertaining, real-life, race-the-clock thriller that nabs you at the start and never makes a wrong move.

author: Manohla Dargis
quote: It's a doozy of a story and so borderline ridiculous that it sounds - ta-da! - like something that could have been cooked up only by Hollywood.

author: Wesley Morris
quote: Argo is absurdly suspenseful for both of its hours. I've never been this stressed-out watching people shred documents.

author: Steven Rea
quote: Argo's white-knuckle nail-biter of a climax takes liberties with how events played out in real life. But while Affleck and screenwriter Chris Terrio have opted to go Hollywood, it's high-class Hollywood, not the low-rent and exploitative route that the make-believe movie at the heart of this tale would have taken.

author: Bob Mondello
quote: By its final fade, Argo feels like more than just a thriller - even a thriller with real thrills and serious Oscar buzz. It feels like a window on events that led to the world we live in now.

author: Kenneth Turan
quote: Affleck easily orchestrates this complex film with 120 speaking parts as it moves from inside-the-Beltway espionage thriller to inside Hollywood dark comedy to gripping international hostage drama, all without missing a step.

author: Peter Travers
quote: Just so we're straight, Ben Affleck doesn't merely direct Argo, he directs the hell out of it, nailing the quickening pace, the wayward humor, the nerve-frying suspense.

author: Alison Willmore
quote: The primary weakness of Affleck's film is the actor himself, who can't seem to find much in "exfiltration" specialist Tony aside from a dedication to his work and sorrow over the potential breakup of his family.

author: Joe Morgenstern
quote: The results are nothing less than sensational.

author: Joe Williams
quote: Before it turns into a great escape flick, Argo is an amusing spoof of the movie biz.

author: Ann Hornaday
quote: This captivating, expertly machined political thriller jumps through every hoop the naysayer can set up: It's serious and substantive, an ingeniously written and executed drama fashioned from a fascinating, little-known chapter of recent history.

author: Claudia Puig
quote: A superbly crafted and darkly funny real-life political thriller, with pitch-perfect performances.

author: Amy Biancolli
quote: The main source of astonishment is the precision exhibited everywhere, from the slyly vintage look of Rodrigo Prieto's cinematography to the gradual, cinching tension in Chris Terrio's careful screenplay.

author: Marc Mohan
quote: The period details are spotless, kindling memories of those days of yellow ribbons and nightly news updates on the fate of the American hostages.

author: Andrew O'Hehir
quote: To Ben Affleck's credit, he's made a terrific, pulse-elevating thriller that will leave the audience cheering.

author: Rene Rodriguez
quote: This is a talky picture, based on a historical incident where the outcome is already known – yet it still proves much more engrossing than crime dramas or bank robberies.

author: Liam Lacey
quote: Argo is a movie of many parts, the sum of which can probably be best described as enjoyable Hollywood hokum.

author: Lou Lumenick
quote: A blue-chip Oscar contender that's also a rousing popcorn movie, Ben Affleck's Argo offers plenty of nail-biting thrills as well as funnier scenes than you'd ever imagine possible in the grim context of the Iran hostage crisis.

author: Steve Persall
quote: Argo works superbly on two levels, first as a white-knuckle re-enactment of events in Iran and scrambling strategies in Washington.

author: James Berardinelli
quote: Recognizing that many of the movie's elements are lifted from actual events elevates the importance of what the movie has to say.

author: Bill Goodykoontz
quote: Simply put, Argo is why we go to movies.

author: Karina Longworth
quote: A "gritty" historical drama overwhelmed by its love of Hollywood as an inventor of imaginary narratives with real consequences, a great generator of American bedtime stories whose magic works on suburban kids and foreign enemies alike.

author: Roger Ebert
quote: Argo the real movie about the fake movie, is both spellbinding and surprisingly funny.

author: Marjorie Baumgarten
quote: Affleck's greatest talent, however, may lie in his casting instincts: In addition to the above-mentioned turns by Arkin and Goodman, stand-out performances are also delivered by Bryan Cranston as Mendez's boss and Victor Garber as the morally heroic Canadian ambassador to Iran.

author: Keith Phipps
quote: Only the finale threatens to undo all that hard work. Though well-done, the last act leans less on the facts of the case than on Hollywood contrivances, heightening the tension with embellishments that feel at odds with the methodical, deliberate film leading up to them.

author: Joshua Rothkopf
quote: No performances stand out, which is a shame given Affleck's track record with actors. Ultimately, it comes down to a chase to the airport, with a scary Revolutionary Guardsman at the gate.

author: Rex Reed
quote: Argo is a triumph. It has tension, sincerity, mystery, artistic responsibility, entertainment value, technical expertise, a narrative arc and a thrilling respect for the tradition of how to tell a story with minimum frills and maximum impact. It's a great footnote to history, one of the best films of 2012 and a sure-fire contender on Oscar night.

author: David Edelstein
quote: A marvel of cunning, an irresistible blend of cool realism and Hollywood hokum.

author: Anthony Lane
quote: Finally, a voice-over from Jimmy Carter, lauding the efforts of those involved. All this is, frankly, uncool - a pity, because the rest of Argo feels clever, taut, and restrained.

author: Owen Gleiberman
quote: Argo is never less than wildly entertaining, but a major part of its power is that it so ominously captures the kickoff to the world we're in now.

author: Richard Corliss
quote: In terms of quality, though, Argo is just so-so.

author: Eric Kohn
quote: Equally a slick political thriller, intelligent period piece and sly Hollywood satire, Ben Affleck's Argo maintains a careful balance between commentary and entertainment value.

author: Rodrigo Perez
quote: Extraordinarily suspenseful, extremely well-told and effortless in its complex tonal balance.

author: Pete Hammond
quote: If there was any doubt Ben Affleck has turned into an exceptional director, his wildly entertaining, pulse-pounding thriller Argo will handily erase those thoughts.

author: Henry Barnes
quote: There's a degree of puffery in the writing, however, that makes this drama untrustworthy.

author: Todd McCarthy
quote: Argo is a crackerjack political thriller told with intelligence, great period detail and a surprising amount of nutty humor for a serious look at the Iran hostage crisis of 1979-81.

author: Peter Debruge
quote: Ultimately, the thrill of Argo is in watching how the illusion-making of movies found such an unlikely application on the world political stage, where the stakes were literally life and death.

author: Donald Clarke
quote: There is a point to all this. As well as offering a delicious audio-visual feast, the film firmly makes the case that those who have least to blame for global warming — those living close to nature — will be the ones who ultimately suffer the most. If we have to be taught such a grim lesson then this is the way to do it.

author: Peter Bradshaw
quote: Beasts of the Southern Wild is a vividly poetic and maybe even therapeutic response to one of the most painful and mortifying episodes in modern American history, second only to 9/11.

author: Damon Wise
quote: Beautiful, funny, timely and tender, this is the American arthouse movie of the year.

author: Kate Stables
